html:
[401]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 至夜而西墙颓 - Literal meaning: 'at night the western wall collapsed.' Metaphorical use suggests a sudden and unexpected event.
  • 岩墙之下 - Literal meaning: 'beneath a cliff or steep wall.' Symbolizes a precarious situation.
  • 榻间蹀躞而谘咀者 - Complex phrase: 'those who pace back and forth in the bedroom, whispering and muttering.'
  • 阴相之 - Means 'to secretly assist' or 'to help from behind the scenes,' often used to describe the actions of spirits or deities.
  • 巧为趋避 - Means 'to skillfully avoid' or 'to cleverly evade,' suggesting deliberate and cunning efforts to escape danger.
  • 卒蹈陷阱而不自知 - Means 'eventually fall into traps without realizing it,' implying a failure to recognize hidden dangers.
  • 鬼头王 - Nickname given to the son of Wáng Mǐn, indicating that he is the head of a ghost.
  • 早起梳沐，必于榻上帷幙中 - Means 'always combs and bathes in the bedchamber behind the curtains,' suggesting a secretive or ritualistic behavior.
  • 伺其自出，然后敢前 - Means 'waits until she comes out, then dares to approach,' indicating a cautious and respectful attitude.
  • 仓皇举头加颈不及，身首俱仆 - Describes a sudden and dramatic action where the headless figure tries to put its head back on but fails, resulting in both body and head falling to the ground.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 金陵指挥王敏 - Refers to Wáng Mǐn, a military officer from Jinling (modern-day Nanjing).
  • 济宁 - A city in Shandong Province, where Wáng Mǐn purchased his concubine.
  • 鬼神实阴相之 - A common belief in Chinese culture that spirits and deities can secretly assist or interfere with human affairs.
  • 生死祸福，有数存焉否耶 - A philosophical question about fate and destiny, reflecting the Chinese belief in the influence of fate on life and death.
  • 世之巧为趋避，卒蹈陷阱而不自知者 - Critiques the idea that people can avoid all dangers through cleverness, suggesting that some risks are inevitable.

Proper Nouns

  • 金陵 - Jinling, now known as Nanjing.
  • 济宁 - Jining, a city in Shandong Province.
  • 王敏 - Wáng Mǐn, the military officer.
  • 鬼头王 - A nickname for Wáng Mǐn's son.

[402]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 此条见盛百二补入《济宁志》 - This phrase indicates that the information was added to the *Jining Gazetteer* by someone named Sheng Bai'er. '此条' means 'this entry,' '见' means 'is seen in,' '补入' means 'added to,' and '《济宁志》' refers to the *Jining Gazetteer*.
  • 金酒缸 - A nickname given to a person, likely referring to a magistrate or official named Jin. The term '酒缸' (wine vat) is used metaphorically to describe his drinking habits, suggesting he was heavily involved in alcohol consumption.
  • 奉调入帘 - This phrase means 'appointed to enter the examination hall,' which was a common term for officials who were sent to oversee the imperial examinations.
  • 摄篆务 - '摄' means 'to take charge of temporarily,' and '篆务' refers to administrative duties. Together, it means 'to temporarily handle administrative affairs.'
  • 当其喜则以糊涂了事 - This idiom means 'when pleased, he would settle matters carelessly.' '糊涂' means 'careless' or 'confused,' and '了事' means 'to settle or finish something.'
  • 其怒则捶楚交下 - This phrase means 'when angry, he would beat people mercilessly.' '捶楚' refers to beating, and '交下' means 'to descend upon' or 'to rain down.'
  • 名曰“金酒缸” - This means 'named him 'Jin Wine Vat.' '名曰' means 'to name as' or 'to call.'
  • 登堂以大瓢置案头 - This phrase describes the scene where Jin placed a large gourd (used for drinking) on the desk when he entered the hall. '登堂' means 'to enter the hall,' and '置案头' means 'to place on the desk.'
  • 堂上翁今满载归 - This phrase is a sarcastic remark, meaning 'the old man in the hall is now returning fully loaded.' '堂上翁' refers to the magistrate, and '满载归' suggests he is drunk or overly satisfied.
  • 真可谓名教之罪人也 - This phrase means 'truly he is a great sinner against proper conduct.' '名教' refers to Confucian moral principles, and '罪人' means 'sinner.'
  • 暴卒 - This means 'died suddenly.' '暴' means 'sudden,' and '卒' means 'death.'
  • 尸未殓 - This means 'the body had not yet been buried.' '尸' means 'body,' and '未殓' means 'not yet buried.'
  • 逻守 - This means 'to guard or watch.' '逻' means 'to patrol,' and '守' means 'to guard.'
  • 席地共饮 - This phrase means 'sat on the ground and drank together.' '席地' means 'to sit on the ground,' and '共饮' means 'to drink together.'
  • 背尸坐 - This means 'sitting with their backs to the body.' '背' means 'back,' and '尸' means 'body.'
  • 西向者 - This refers to 'the one facing west.' '西' means 'west,' and '向' means 'facing.'
  • 见尸下 - This means 'saw the body rise.' '见' means 'saw,' and '尸下' means 'the body rose.'
  • 群舁之归 - This means 'carried him back collectively.' '群' means 'collectively,' and '舁之归' means 'carried him back.'
  • 挂锞哀杖 - This refers to 'a ceremonial stick used for beating.' '挂锞' refers to a ceremonial object, and '哀杖' means 'a stick for beating.'
  • 牢不可破 - This means 'firmly fixed and unbreakable.' '牢' means 'firmly,' and '不可破' means 'unbreakable.'
  • 喉间 - This means 'throat.' '喉' means 'throat,' and '间' means 'in.'
  • 呃于嗓 - This means 'stuck in the throat.' '呃' means 'stuck,' and '嗓' means 'throat.'
  • 为之抉出 - This means 'removed it.' '为' means 'for,' '之' means 'it,' and '抉出' means 'to remove.'
  • 而气亦不复续 - This means 'and his breath also did not continue.' '而' means 'and,' '气' means 'breath,' '亦' means 'also,' and '不复续' means 'did not continue.'
  • 探其喉间 - This means 'examined his throat.' '探' means 'examined,' and '喉间' means 'throat.'
  • 有一小粗磁酒杯 - This means 'there was a small, rough ceramic wine cup.' '有' means 'there was,' '一小' means 'small,' '粗' means 'rough,' '磁' means 'ceramic,' and '酒杯' means 'wine cup.'
  • 贪主人之馀沥者 - This means 'someone who coveted the owner's leftover liquor.' '贪' means 'coveted,' '主人' means 'owner,' '之' means 'of,' '馀沥' means 'leftover liquor,' and '者' means 'person.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 盛百二 - This refers to Sheng Bai'er, a person who added this entry to the *Jining Gazetteer*. The *Jining Gazetteer* is a historical record of the city of Jining in Shandong Province.
  • 济宁志 - The *Jining Gazetteer* is a local history book that documents the events, people, and places of Jining.
  • 奉调入帘 - This term refers to officials who were appointed to oversee the imperial examinations, which were held in the examination halls (帘).
  • 摄篆务 - This term refers to the temporary handling of administrative duties, often by officials who were not in their permanent positions.
  • 名教 - This term refers to Confucian moral principles, which were highly valued in traditional Chinese society.
  • 暴卒 - This term refers to sudden death, often implying an unexpected or mysterious cause.
  • 尸未殓 - This term refers to the state of a body that has not yet been buried, which was a common practice in traditional Chinese funerary customs.
  • 逻守 - This term refers to the act of guarding or watching, often in the context of protecting a place or person.
  • 席地共饮 - This term refers to the practice of sitting on the ground and drinking together, which was a common social activity in traditional Chinese culture.
  • 背尸坐 - This term refers to the act of sitting with one's back to a body, which could be interpreted as a sign of disrespect or fear.
  • 西向者 - This term refers to the direction of west, which was often associated with the afterlife or the underworld in Chinese culture.
  • 见尸下 - This term refers to the act of seeing a body rise, which could be interpreted as a supernatural event or a sign of possession.
  • 群舁之归 - This term refers to the act of carrying someone back collectively, which could be interpreted as a sign of respect or a response to a crisis.
  • 挂锞哀杖 - This term refers to a ceremonial stick used for beating, which was often used in traditional Chinese rituals.
  • 牢不可破 - This term refers to something that is firmly fixed and unbreakable, which could be interpreted as a sign of strength or a curse.
  • 喉间 - This term refers to the throat, which was often associated with speech, breath, and life in Chinese culture.
  • 呃于嗓 - This term refers to something that is stuck in the throat, which could be interpreted as a sign of choking or a curse.
  • 为之抉出 - This term refers to the act of removing something, which could be interpreted as a sign of relief or a curse being lifted.
  • 而气亦不复续 - This term refers to the act of breathing stopping, which could be interpreted as a sign of death or a curse taking effect.
  • 探其喉间 - This term refers to the act of examining the throat, which could be interpreted as a sign of investigation or a curse being revealed.
  • 有一小粗磁酒杯 - This term refers to a small, rough ceramic wine cup, which could be interpreted as a sign of poverty or a curse.
  • 贪主人之馀沥者 - This term refers to someone who coveted the owner's leftover liquor, which could be interpreted as a sign of greed or a curse.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sentence is a straightforward statement of fact, but the phrase '见盛百二补入' could be interpreted as either 'seen by Sheng Bai'er' or 'added by Sheng Bai'er.'
  • This nickname is a metaphorical description of Jin's drinking habits, but it could be interpreted as either a literal description or a metaphor.

[403]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 邑庠生 - Refers to a student in a local school or academy, often used to denote someone of lower scholarly rank.
  • 魂游于野 - Literally means 'soul wandering in the wild,' indicating a state of spiritual wandering or detachment.
  • 孑然独行 - Means 'walking alone,' emphasizing solitude.
  • 衣无下体 - Means 'no lower garment,' describing a state of undress or partial nudity.
  • 徘徊于石桥危磴之间 - Means 'wandering between the stone bridge and dangerous steps,' indicating a place of uncertainty or risk.
  • 环珮璆然 - Describes the sound of jade ornaments, indicating elegance or nobility.
  • 翩跹而来 - Means 'gracefully coming,' emphasizing a dignified and elegant manner.
  • 贵家娃 - Refers to a young woman from a wealthy family.
  • 侍婢一人前导 - Means 'a maid servant leading the way,' indicating a retinue or entourage.
  • 自惭形秽 - Means 'ashamed of one's appearance,' indicating a feeling of inadequacy.
  • 避之桥下 - Means 'hid under the bridge,' indicating a place of refuge.
  • 敛衽 - Means 'to gather one's skirt,' a gesture of respect or greeting.
  • 嫂何至此 - Means 'what brings you here, sister?' indicating a question of curiosity or concern.
  • 久将神不返舍矣 - Means 'long have you not returned to your body,' indicating a prolonged absence from the physical world.
  • 两世隔绝 - Means 'separated by two lifetimes,' indicating a significant gap in time or existence.
  • 与兄固途人也 - Means 'we are strangers to each other,' indicating a lack of prior acquaintance.
  • 兄固不识弟 - Means 'you certainly don't know me,' indicating a lack of recognition.
  • 且弟又隔世为女 - Means 'and I am a woman from a different era,' indicating a change in identity and time.
  • 借嫂舍以探兄耶 - Means 'to borrow your home to see my brother,' indicating a request for permission or hospitality.
  • 盍假舍以见吾朱兄 - Means 'why not let me stay here to see my brother Zhu,' indicating a suggestion or invitation.
  • 深爱兄数首诗 - Means 'deeply love your several poems,' indicating admiration for the person's literary works.
  • 惓惓不能去诸怀 - Means 'cannot forget them from my heart,' indicating a strong and lasting memory.
  • 记四首诗 - Means 'remember four poems,' indicating specific literary works.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 朱广 - A name, likely referring to a historical figure or a character in a story.
  • 邑庠生 - A term for a local school student, indicating a lower scholarly rank.
  • 魂游于野 - A concept indicating a state of spiritual wandering, often associated with ghosts or spirits.
  • 石桥危磴 - Refers to a stone bridge and dangerous steps, indicating a place of physical and metaphorical peril.
  • 环珮璆然 - A description of jade ornaments, often associated with nobility and elegance.
  • 翩跹而来 - A description of graceful movement, often associated with noble or refined individuals.
  • 贵家娃 - Refers to a young woman from a wealthy family, indicating social status.
  • 侍婢一人前导 - Indicates a retinue or entourage, often associated with nobility.
  • 自惭形秽 - A feeling of inadequacy, often associated with social or moral inferiority.
  • 敛衽 - A gesture of respect or greeting, often associated with formal or ritual contexts.
  • 嫂何至此 - A question of curiosity or concern, often associated with familial relationships.
  • 久将神不返舍矣 - Indicates a prolonged absence from the physical world, often associated with spiritual or supernatural phenomena.
  • 两世隔绝 - Indicates a significant gap in time or existence, often associated with reincarnation or otherworldly concepts.
  • 与兄固途人也 - Indicates a lack of prior acquaintance, often associated with social or familial relationships.
  • 兄固不识弟 - Indicates a lack of recognition, often associated with social or familial relationships.
  • 且弟又隔世为女 - Indicates a change in identity and time, often associated with reincarnation or otherworldly concepts.
  • 借嫂舍以探兄耶 - Indicates a request for permission or hospitality, often associated with social or familial relationships.
  • 盍假舍以见吾朱兄 - Indicates a suggestion or invitation, often associated with social or familial relationships.
  • 深爱兄数首诗 - Indicates admiration for the person's literary works, often associated with literary or artistic circles.
  • 惓惓不能去诸怀 - Indicates a strong and lasting memory, often associated with personal or emotional connections.
  • 记四首诗 - Indicates specific literary works, often associated with literary or artistic circles.

Proper Nouns

  • 朱广 - A name, likely referring to a historical figure or a character in a story.
  • 张氏 - A name, likely referring to the protagonist's wife.

[404]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 探梅 - Classical term meaning 'to explore or seek plum blossoms,' often used in poetry to describe the act of searching for plum blossoms in winter.
  • 琼瑶碎踏兴偏奢 - Metaphorical description of walking through snow or ice, suggesting a luxurious and pleasurable experience.
  • 几回路暗初无迹 - Describes a path that is initially indistinct or invisible, suggesting a journey through a challenging or mysterious environment.
  • 不断香蒸何处花 - Uses a metaphor to describe the continuous fragrance rising from an unknown source, creating a sense of mystery and beauty.
  • 人到岭头纤月落 - Combines the physical act of reaching a high point with the visual imagery of a setting moon, creating a vivid and evocative scene.
  • 神传竹外一枝斜 - Suggests a mystical or divine transmission of a single branch leaning outside the bamboo, adding a spiritual dimension to the natural setting.
  • 未知持赠伊谁好 - Expresses uncertainty about the best recipient for a gift, reflecting a thoughtful and considerate attitude.
  • 欲寄遥情天水涯 - Describes the desire to send far-reaching emotions to the farthest reaches of the horizon, emphasizing the depth and breadth of the feelings.
  • 赏梅 - Classical term meaning 'to appreciate plum blossoms,' often used in poetry to describe the act of admiring plum blossoms.
  • 孤山选胜白云乡 - Describes the selection of the most beautiful place in a scenic area, highlighting the beauty and serenity of the location.
  • 何逊幽怀此寄将 - Suggests that deep feelings are being conveyed through poetry, reflecting the emotional depth of the poet.
  • 淡处还如僧入定 - Compares the quiet and serene aspects of the scene to the state of deep meditation, creating a sense of inner peace.
  • 夜深浑觉月生香 - Describes the strong sensation of the moon emitting fragrance, creating a mystical and evocative atmosphere.
  • 亭亭瘦影思无邪 - Describes a tall and elegant form with a thin and delicate shadow, suggesting innocent and pure thoughts.
  • 寂寂寒芳味正长 - Describes a peaceful and silent atmosphere with the fragrance of cold flowers, suggesting that the fragrance is enduring.
  • 今日断桥春尚早 - Indicates that spring has not yet fully arrived, describing the early spring season.
  • 正披风帽过雷塘 - Describes the action of putting on a wind hat and passing by a specific location, adding a sense of movement and activity.
  • 惜梅 - Classical term meaning 'to cherish or lament plum blossoms,' often used in poetry to express regret or admiration for plum blossoms.
  • 冰胎结到此时成 - Suggests a frozen form that has formed by now, describing the current state of the environment.
  • 褪尽繁华却有卿 - Suggests that despite the loss of beauty and richness, there is still someone special, reflecting a sense of resilience and hope.
  • 入画丰神曾作态 - Describes a graceful and elegant presence that was once posed for a painting, adding a historical and artistic dimension.
  • 坠楼时节总无声 - Describes a tragic event that is always silent, emphasizing the somber and reflective nature of the scene.
  • 书传陇上相思恨 - Suggests that letters carry messages of longing and resentment, reflecting deep emotional connections.
  • 梦绕江南逆旅情 - Describes dreams that wander and the feelings of a traveler, expressing a sense of nostalgia and wanderlust.
  • 惆怅岁寒踪迹少 - Describes a sad and pensive mood during the cold season, suggesting that there are few signs of activity.
  • 漫劳仙客竟呼兄 - Suggests that the effort was futile, and the mythical figure finally addresses someone as a brother, adding a mystical and emotional layer.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 探梅 - The act of seeking plum blossoms is a common theme in Chinese poetry, often symbolizing the pursuit of beauty and purity.
  • 孤山 - Located in Hangzhou, China, is a famous scenic spot known for its plum blossoms and is often referenced in poetry.
  • 何逊 - A renowned poet from the Southern Dynasties period, known for his elegant and refined poetry.
  • 断桥 - A famous bridge in Hangzhou, often associated with romantic and poetic imagery.
  • 雷塘 - A lake in Hangzhou, often mentioned in poetry for its scenic beauty.
  • 江南 - The southern region of China, known for its picturesque landscapes and cultural significance.

[405]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 作幕者多无后 - Common idiom meaning 'those who serve as secretaries often have no descendants.'
  • 刑名法术 - Refers to legal and administrative techniques.
  • 草菅人命 - Means 'to treat human lives as weeds,' referring to callous disregard for human life.
  • 倒置是非 - Means 'to reverse right and wrong,' indicating a moral or ethical lapse.
  • 掌珠 - A metaphor for a beloved daughter.
  • 天罚其嗣 - Means 'punished by heaven for not having descendants.'
  • 幕字本草 - A playful reference to a dictionary or compendium of terms related to serving as a secretary.
  • 性寒，有毒 - Metaphorical descriptions of the 'nature' of secretarial work, suggesting it is cold and potentially harmful.
  • 味微酸 - Describes the taste as slightly sour, again metaphorically.
  • 无种 - Means 'no seed' or 'no offspring,' reinforcing the idea that secretaries do not have descendants.
  • 晚生 - Means 'late-born,' suggesting that the work of a secretary is not immediately fruitful.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 岱宗 - Another name for Mount Tai, one of the Five Great Mountains of China, symbolizing the center of the world.
  • 衡山 - One of the Five Great Mountains, known for its height and spiritual significance.
  • 华山 - Another of the Five Great Mountains, famous for its steep cliffs and challenging climbs.
  • 恒山 - One of the Five Great Mountains, known for its historical and cultural importance.
  • 嵩山 - One of the Five Great Mountains, known for its religious sites and natural beauty.

Proper Nouns

  • 岱宗 - Another name for Mount Tai.
  • 衡山 - A specific mountain.
  • 华山 - A specific mountain.
  • 恒山 - A specific mountain.
  • 嵩山 - A specific mountain.
  • 雨亭 - A person's name, likely referring to a friend of the author.
  • 掌珠 - A metaphorical term for a beloved daughter.

[406]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 少负奇气 - Means 'young and full of extraordinary spirit,' describing the character's youthful vigor and uniqueness.
  • 恣情山水 - Means 'to indulge in the enjoyment of mountains and rivers,' indicating a life dedicated to the appreciation of natural beauty.
  • 搜罗岩谷 - Means 'to search and gather treasures from the mountains and valleys,' suggesting a quest for natural wonders or hidden gems.
  • 参订方舆 - Means 'to study and verify geographical records,' referring to the scholarly pursuit of understanding and mapping the land.
  • 有名山美女癣 - A playful or whimsical phrase, possibly a pun or a personal touch, translating to 'famous mountains, beautiful women, and a skin condition,' which might be a humorous or self-deprecating reference.
  • 洞壑崇峦，层见叠出 - Means 'deep valleys and towering peaks, appearing in layers and succession,' describing the intricate and layered landscapes of the miniature landscape.
  • 谛观三月，难穷其奥境 - Means 'to observe carefully for three months, yet still find it hard to fathom its profound mysteries,' emphasizing the depth and complexity of the object.
  • 雕以檀坐，贮之锦囊 - Means 'carved with a sandalwood seat, stored in a brocade bag,' describing the care and respect given to the object.
  • 竟不令他人见 - Means 'yet never allowing others to see it,' indicating the character's possessiveness and secrecy regarding the object.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 湘陵 - Refers to Xiangling, a place name in China, likely indicating the character's origin.
  • 灵隐寺 - Refers to Lingyin Temple, a famous Buddhist temple in Hangzhou, Zhejiang Province, known for its scenic beauty and cultural significance.
  • 西湖 - Refers to West Lake, a renowned lake in Hangzhou, famous for its picturesque scenery and historical importance.
  • 僧寮 - Refers to a monk's room or quarters within a temple, where religious activities take place.
  • 庋笔物 - Refers to a pen holder or a container for pens, which in this context is a unique and valuable object.
  • 山中古冢旁 - Refers to a place near an ancient tomb in the mountains, suggesting a mysterious and ancient origin.
  • 宝莫能名 - Means 'a treasure beyond naming,' indicating the object's incomparable value and uniqueness.
  • 檀坐 - Refers to a sandalwood seat, a high-quality material often used in Chinese art and furniture.
  • 锦囊 - Refers to a brocade bag, a luxurious and protective storage container.
  • 鲛人获径寸之珠 - Refers to a mermaid obtaining a pearl the size of an inch, another metaphor for possessing something rare and valuable.

Proper Nouns

  • 湘陵熊孝泉 - The name of the character, indicating his place of origin and personal name.
  • 灵隐寺 - The name of the temple where the story takes place.
  • 西湖 - The name of the lake, a significant location in the story.
  • 僧寮 - The name of the monk's room where the object is found.
  • 山中古冢旁 - The location where the object was originally found, adding a layer of mystery to its origin.

[407]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 会当月夕 - Sets the time as a moonlit night, evoking a romantic or mysterious atmosphere.
  • 有款户声 - Literally means 'there was a knocking sound at the door,' but can also imply someone is trying to gain entry.
  • 嫣然一女子入 - Describes a beautiful woman entering, with '嫣然' indicating her charming and graceful appearance.
  • 华妆妙丽 - Refers to her being dressed in elaborate and beautiful attire.
  • 婉而多风 - Describes her demeanor as gentle and full of charm.
  • 劫坟贼今得之矣 - A somewhat cryptic statement that could mean 'the grave robber has been caught' or 'the grave robber has been found.'
  • 从未见夤夜入室，反诬良人为盗者 - A playful response indicating that he has never seen someone enter his room at night and accuse him of being a thief.
  • 汝怀中者，是吾旧家物 - Means 'the thing in your possession is from my former home.'
  • 此一品非耶 - A rhetorical question meaning 'isn't this one of them?'
  • 捉襟已失 - Means 'the item was already gone when I checked my clothes.'
  • 失而得之，不幸之幸 - A paradoxical statement meaning 'losing and finding it is both unfortunate and fortunate.'
  • 囊裳欲去 - Means 'she was about to leave with her clothes.'
  • 曳女裙，不令出 - Means 'he pulled her skirt to prevent her from leaving.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 会当月夕 - Evokes a traditional setting where moonlit nights are often associated with romance and mystery.
  • 劫坟贼 - Refers to grave robbers, a common theme in Chinese literature and folklore.
  • 华妆妙丽 - Reflects the beauty standards and fashion of the time.
  • 婉而多风 - Describes a woman's demeanor in a way that aligns with traditional Chinese aesthetics of grace and charm.
  • 劫坟贼今得之矣 - Implies a resolution to a crime, which is a common narrative element in stories of the time.
  • 从未见夤夜入室，反诬良人为盗者 - Reflects a playful and defensive tone, typical in social interactions.
  • 此一品非耶 - Uses a rhetorical question to challenge the claim, a common conversational tactic.
  • 失而得之，不幸之幸 - Reflects a philosophical view on loss and gain, common in Chinese thought.
  • 囊裳欲去 - Implies a quick and stealthy departure, often used in narratives of escape or evasion.
  • 曳女裙，不令出 - Describes a physical action that is both forceful and intimate, reflecting a moment of tension and attraction.

Proper Nouns

  • 熊 - The male protagonist's name.
  • 小罗浮 - A specific item or object, possibly a miniature landscape or a piece of art.
  • 铁桥 - A specific feature or element.
  • 老人 - An elderly figure or symbol.
  • 大、小旗 - Large and small flags or banners.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The opening sequence '正凝思间，双影齐下，若闻诟谇，生不敢置喙' sets the scene: the protagonist is lost in thought when two figures appear, and he hears criticism but remains silent. The sentence is elliptical, with the subject (the protagonist) implied.
  • The sentence '二兰云' shifts to the perspective of the two Lanes, who express disapproval of the situation. The subject (the two Lanes) is clear, but the verb '云' (said) is used without a full sentence.
  • The sentence '左青云' shifts again to the perspective of Left Qing, who expresses a different opinion. The subject (Left Qing) is clear, but the verb '云' (said) is used without a full sentence.
  • The sequence '遂两袭其裾，同登卧榻，共相偎倚' describes the physical actions of the protagonist and the two Lanes. The sentence is elliptical, with the subject (the protagonist) implied.
  • The sequence '生欲与二兰致情，左青隔，不使通' describes the protagonist's attempt to express his feelings, which is blocked by Left Qing. The sentence is elliptical, with the subject (the protagonist) implied.
  • The sequence '事齐不可，事楚又不可，悉索交敝，终夜不宁' describes the protagonist's internal conflict and the resulting restlessness. The sentence is elliptical, with the subject (the protagonist) implied.
  • The sequence '欲树静而风转摇之，调停向背，位致大小，各不相亚' describes the protagonist's attempts to maintain peace, but external forces keep disrupting it. The sentence is elliptical, with the subject (the protagonist) implied.
  • The sequence '口角之间，未尝不絮絮然当以旗鼓' describes the ongoing verbal or emotional competition between the women. The sentence is elliptical, with the subject (the women) implied.

  • 疑是猪妖 - Literally means 'suspected to be a pig demon,' referring to a supernatural being with pig-like characteristics.

  • 杨汝虔，为银商，开生矿，家暴富，得银之磄也 - Describes Yang Ruqian as a silver merchant who became extremely wealthy by mining silver. '家暴富' means 'became suddenly rich,' and '得银之磄也' suggests he achieved this through hard work or luck.
  • 族无缙绅，时见凌于官长 - Indicates that Yang's family lacked influential officials (缙绅) and was often mistreated by local authorities.

  • 抵都，假寓于珠宝市 - Means 'upon arriving in the capital, he rented a place to live in the jewelry market.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 返寓易其金，南归，攻举业，不复言画 - '返寓' means 'return to the residence,' '易其金' means 'exchange the gold,' and '南归' means 'return to the south.' '攻举业' means 'to pursue scholarly studies,' and '不复言画' means 'no longer talk about painting.'
  • 时悬女照于帏中，如临师保 - '时悬女照于帏中' means 'at times, hang the girl's portrait in the curtain,' and '如临师保' means 'as if in the presence of a teacher or guardian.'
  • 往往读罢对之而哭，哭罢复读 - '往往' means 'often,' '读罢对之而哭' means 'after reading, cry in front of it,' and '哭罢复读' means 'after crying, read again.'
  • 后入京师，乡、会联捷，入词林 - '乡、会联捷' means 'success in both the provincial and metropolitan examinations,' and '入词林' means 'enter the literary establishment.'
  • 有山东张进士者，与生同校录，知生未娶，欲以桂姓表妹妻生 - '张进士' means 'Mr. Zhang, a successful candidate in the imperial examination,' '同校录' means 'work together in the same office,' '知生未娶' means 'know that the student is unmarried,' and '欲以桂姓表妹妻生' means 'wants to marry his cousin named Gui to the student.'
  • 生问里居，果荣城桂守之女，异之，遂许婚 - '生问里居' means 'the student asks about her place of origin,' '果荣城桂守之女' means 'indeed, she is the daughter of the governor of Rongcheng,' '异之' means 'finds it extraordinary,' and '遂许婚' means 'agrees to the marriage.'
  • 桂公任满，告休入都，见中表张庶常，托为择婚，恐荣城僻地，无可坦者，并留女于都 - '桂公任满' means 'Governor Gui's term is over,' '告休入都' means 'retires and goes to the capital,' '见中表张庶常' means 'meets his cousin Zhang, a successful candidate in the imperial examination,' '托为择婚' means 'asks him to find a suitable match,' '恐荣城僻地，无可坦者' means 'fears that Rongcheng is a remote place with no suitable prospects,' and '并留女于都' means 'and leaves his daughter in the capital.'
  • 一日，女与诸姊出奇华门，游二闸，泛小舟 - '一日' means 'one day,' '女与诸姊出奇华门' means 'the girl and her sisters go out through the Qihua Gate,' '游二闸' means 'visit the Second Sluice,' and '泛小舟' means 'row a small boat.'
  • 忽岸旁一女子呼共济，舟近载之 - '忽岸旁一女子呼共济' means 'suddenly, a woman on the shore calls to join the boat,' and '舟近载之' means 'the boat approaches and takes her on board.'
  • 女登舟，欻然不见，众以为怪 - '女登舟，欻然不见' means 'the girl boards the boat and suddenly disappears,' and '众以为怪' means 'everyone finds it strange.'
  • 归，女之丰神顿改，灵敏异常 - '归' means 'returns,' '女之丰神顿改' means 'the girl's appearance suddenly changes,' and '灵敏异常' means 'becomes extraordinarily sharp-witted.'
  • 女先通书词，今一时造艺，殊堪刮目 - '女先通书词' means 'the girl was already well-versed in letters,' and '今一时造艺，殊堪刮目' means 'now, in a short time, she has developed remarkable skills, truly worthy of admiration.'
  • 文词书籍，考之不能屈 - '文词书籍' means 'literary works and books,' and '考之不能屈' means 'cannot be challenged or refuted.'
  • 至涓吉，张公主婚，行亲迎礼 - '至涓吉' means 'when the auspicious day is chosen,' '张公主婚' means 'Mr. Zhang oversees the marriage,' and '行亲迎礼' means 'the bridegroom personally comes to fetch the bride.'
  • 生笑应之 - '生笑应之' means 'the student smiles and responds.'
  • 偕归交拜，揭面盖，视女与非非无异，心知其非非非也 - '偕归交拜' means 'they return together and bow to each other,' '揭面盖' means 'lift the veil,' '视女与非非无异' means 'see that the girl is no different from the ghost,' and '心知其非非非也' means 'but the student knows that she is not the same ghost.'
  • 然不知今日之非非，果有异于前日之非非否也 - '然不知今日之非非，果有异于前日之非非否也' means 'but the student does not know if today's ghost is truly different from yesterday's ghost.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 幸有贻金，可以办行装 - The phrase '贻金' (gift of gold) is a metaphor for a valuable gift, often used in classical texts to denote a significant contribution.
  • 返寓易其金，南归，攻举业，不复言画 - '攻举业' refers to the pursuit of scholarly studies, particularly for the imperial examinations.
  • 时悬女照于帏中，如临师保 - '师保' refers to a teacher or guardian, emphasizing the girl's importance and the student's respect for her.
  • 往往读罢对之而哭，哭罢复读 - This reflects the deep emotional connection and the student's dedication to the girl's memory.
  • 后入京师，乡、会联捷，入词林 - '乡、会联捷' refers to success in both the provincial and metropolitan examinations, which were crucial for entering the literary establishment.
  • 有山东张进士者，与生同校录，知生未娶，欲以桂姓表妹妻生 - '张进士' refers to a successful candidate in the imperial examination, and '桂姓表妹' refers to a cousin from the Gui family.
  • 生问里居，果荣城桂守之女，异之，遂许婚 - '荣城桂守之女' refers to the daughter of the governor of Rongcheng, indicating her high social status.
  • 桂公任满，告休入都，见中表张庶常，托为择婚，恐荣城僻地，无可坦者，并留女于都 - '桂公任满' refers to the completion of Governor Gui's term, and '中表张庶常' refers to a cousin who is a successful candidate in the imperial examination.
  • 一日，女与诸姊出奇华门，游二闸，泛小舟 - '奇华门' and '二闸' are specific locations in the capital, reflecting the girl's social activities.
  • 忽岸旁一女子呼共济，舟近载之 - This scene describes a supernatural event, where a ghost boards the boat.
  • 女登舟，欻然不见，众以为怪 - The sudden disappearance of the girl is a supernatural element, indicating her ghostly nature.
  • 归，女之丰神顿改，灵敏异常 - The girl's transformation upon returning is a key element of the supernatural story.
  • 女先通书词，今一时造艺，殊堪刮目 - This highlights the girl's rapid development of skills, which is unusual and noteworthy.
  • 文词书籍，考之不能屈 - This emphasizes the girl's exceptional knowledge and skills.
  • 至涓吉，张公主婚，行亲迎礼 - '涓吉' refers to choosing an auspicious day for the wedding, and '亲迎礼' refers to the traditional wedding ceremony where the bridegroom personally comes to fetch the bride.
  • 生笑应之 - '生笑应之' means 'the student smiles and responds.'
  • 偕归交拜，揭面盖，视女与非非无异，心知其非非非也 - '偕归交拜' means 'they return together and bow to each other,' '揭面盖' means 'lift the veil,' '视女与非非无异' means 'see that the girl is no different from the ghost,' and '心知其非非非也' means 'but the student knows that she is not the same ghost.'
  • 然不知今日之非非，果有异于前日之非非否也 - '然不知今日之非非，果有异于前日之非非否也' means 'but the student does not know if today's ghost is truly different from yesterday's ghost.'

[439]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 花烛之夕 - Refers to the wedding night, a traditional term in Chinese culture.
  • 共女入帏 - Means 'entering the curtain together with the bride,' symbolizing the consummation of marriage.
  • 画则犹是也 - Literally means 'the painting is still the same,' but implies a contrast with reality.
  • 恐黄金费尽矣 - Means 'fear that all the gold will be spent,' suggesting a concern about the cost or value.
  • 是非非耶？非非非耶？ - A play on words using the character '非' (not), which can be interpreted as a rhetorical question about identity or reality.
  • 非非苟非我，我何以知非非？ - A complex sentence using '非非' (not not) to express a paradoxical or philosophical idea, questioning how one can know something that is not not oneself.
  • 我诚非非非，我固知非非也 - Another use of '非非' to express a deeper philosophical or existential reflection.
  • 夜雨联床 - Refers to a romantic or intimate moment shared by the couple, often associated with poetry and literature.
  • 楼头课读 - Means 'studying together on the upper floor of a building,' indicating a shared intellectual pursuit.
  • 山谷遗金 - Refers to a story or metaphor about hidden treasures in the mountains and valleys, often used to symbolize unexpected wealth or opportunities.
  • 鹏程万里 - A metaphor from the *Zhuangzi* meaning 'a journey of ten thousand miles,' symbolizing a long and successful career or life path.
  • 璧合一双 - Means 'a pair of jade discs fitting together perfectly,' symbolizing a harmonious and perfect union.
  • 终以二心歧视 - Means 'eventually discriminated against due to two hearts,' suggesting a lack of trust or loyalty.
  • 窃恐非非一去，非非复来，而非非则诚非矣 - A complex sentence using '非非' to express a fear that something that is not not will leave and not return, and that '非非' is indeed not.
  • 不敢置喙 - Means 'dared not speak a word,' indicating a sense of awe or reverence.
  • 后女归荣城 - Means 'later, the woman returned to Rongcheng,' indicating a return to her hometown or origin.
  • 闺中幼小时事 - Refers to childhood memories and experiences in the inner quarters of a woman's home.
  • 无不记忆 - Means 'nothing was forgotten,' indicating a strong and clear memory.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 花烛之夕 - A traditional Chinese wedding custom where the couple spends their first night together.
  • 共女入帏 - Symbolizes the consummation of marriage and the beginning of a new life together.
  • 画则犹是也，恐黄金费尽矣 - Suggests a concern about the cost or value of something, possibly a metaphor for the cost of love or commitment.
  • 是非非耶？非非非耶？ - A philosophical or rhetorical question about identity or reality, often used in classical Chinese literature.
  • 非非苟非我，我何以知非非？ - A complex philosophical statement questioning the nature of self and reality.
  • 我诚非非非，我固知非非也 - A deeper philosophical reflection on the nature of self and reality.
  • 夜雨联床 - A romantic or intimate moment often associated with poetry and literature.
  • 楼头课读 - A shared intellectual pursuit, often symbolizing a deep connection between partners.
  • 山谷遗金 - A metaphor for unexpected wealth or opportunities, often used in classical Chinese literature.
  • 鹏程万里 - A metaphor from the *Zhuangzi* symbolizing a long and successful career or life path.
  • 璧合一双 - A symbol of a harmonious and perfect union.
  • 终以二心歧视 - Suggests a lack of trust or loyalty, often used in classical Chinese literature to describe a failing relationship.
  • 窃恐非非一去，非非复来，而非非则诚非矣 - A complex philosophical statement expressing a fear of loss and the nature of reality.
  • 不敢置喙 - Indicates a sense of awe or reverence, often used in classical Chinese literature.
  • 后女归荣城 - Indicates a return to the woman's hometown or origin, often used in classical Chinese literature to describe a return to one's roots.
  • 闺中幼小时事 - Refers to childhood memories and experiences, often used in classical Chinese literature to describe a woman's past.

Proper Nouns

  • 花烛之夕 - The wedding night.
  • 荣城 - The name of the woman's hometown or origin.
  • 非非 - A character used in the text to express a philosophical or rhetorical idea.
  • 鹏程万里 - A metaphor from the *Zhuangzi* symbolizing a long and successful career or life path.
  • 璧合一双 - A symbol of a harmonious and perfect union.
  • 山谷遗金 - A metaphor for unexpected wealth or opportunities.
  • 夜雨联床 - A romantic or intimate moment often associated with poetry and literature.
  • 楼头课读 - A shared intellectual pursuit, often symbolizing a deep connection between partners.
  • 闺中幼小时事 - Refers to childhood memories and experiences in the inner quarters of a woman's home.

[440]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 文笔甚奇警 - Refers to writing that is striking, unusual, or highly expressive; often implies innovation or boldness in style.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 铁岭高少司寇其佩 - Refers to Gao Qipei, a high-ranking official from Tieling (modern-day Liaoning Province), who held the title of '少司寇' (a judicial official).
  • 古狂 - A self-proclaimed title meaning 'ancient madman,' reflecting Gao's eccentric and unconventional personality.
  • 指画 - A unique form of painting where the artist uses their fingers instead of brushes; attributed to Gao Qipei and considered innovative at the time.
  • 渴笔 - A style of calligraphy or painting characterized by dry, sparse strokes, often associated with a sense of urgency or spontaneity.

Proper Nouns

  • 铁岭 - A historical region in modern-day Liaoning Province, China.
  • 高少司寇其佩 - The full name of Gao Qipei, including his official title.

[441]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 公于唐宋元明诸大家中钻研探讨 - The subject has studied and explored the works of major authors from the Tang, Song, Yuan, and Ming dynasties, emphasizing deep engagement with classical literature.
  • 集其大成 - Literally means 'to gather the essence' or 'to synthesize the best,' indicating a comprehensive mastery of the styles and techniques of these authors.
  • 允称独步 - Means 'truly deserves the title of unparalleled,' highlighting the subject's exceptional status in the field of literature.
  • 其章法不拘前人 - The subject's compositional methods are not bound by traditional constraints, suggesting innovation.
  • 主客阴阳，自有阅历真境 - '主客阴阳' refers to the interplay of dominant and subordinate elements in a balanced way, often used in literary composition. '自有阅历真境' suggests that the subject has genuine experience and insight, leading to a deeper understanding of literary creation.
  • 其指法则各指有单用、双用、三指、满手、拳用之异 - Describes the subject's use of finger techniques in painting, which vary in application (single, double, triple, full hand, fist).
  • 其染法则青、赭、红、黄，随意烘用，皆有神味趣机 - Refers to the subject's use of color (blue, ochre, red, yellow) in painting, applied freely to create a sense of spirit, flavor, and interest.
  • 其皴法则披麻、荷叶、斧劈，各有巨细，难名其妙 - Describes the subject's use of various皴法 (textural techniques) in painting, such as '披麻' (wrinkled like hemp), '荷叶' (like lotus leaves), and '斧劈' (like axe cuts), each with its own variations and subtleties.
  • 其用色如胭脂宜淡而偏浓、赭不宜赤而偏重，青绿加于重墨、硃粉施之金箑，皆古人之所不敢 - Describes the subject's innovative use of color, such as using light shades of rouge but preferring darker tones, ochre that is not too red but heavy, and adding blue-green to dark ink or applying red powder to gold fans—methods that were unconventional for ancient painters.
  • 其用墨至五色而无痕，于无痕而有象，尤觉自然 - The subject's use of ink reaches a level where it appears to have five colors but is seamless, and yet it still conveys form and imagery, making it seem particularly natural.
  • 故见公画者，莫不知其天资高、学力到、胸襟阔大也 - Concludes that anyone who sees the subject's paintings will recognize his high natural talent, extensive learning, and broad-mindedness.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 唐宋元明诸大家 - Refers to major literary figures from the Tang, Song, Yuan, and Ming dynasties, a period known for its rich literary heritage.
  • 章法 - Refers to compositional methods in literature or art, particularly in classical Chinese poetry and prose.
  • 指法 - Refers to techniques in calligraphy or painting, specifically the use of fingers.
  • 染法 - Refers to coloring techniques in painting.
  • 皴法 - Refers to textural techniques in painting, used to depict the roughness of surfaces.

Proper Nouns

  • 唐宋元明 - Refers to the Tang, Song, Yuan, and Ming dynasties.
  • 诸大家 - Refers to major literary figures.

[442]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 公画钟进士像，不下二百馀本 - 公 (Mr.) refers to the artist, 画 (paint) means to create an image, 钟进士像 (Zong Jinshi's portrait) is the subject of the painting, 不下二百馀本 (not less than two hundred copies) indicates the number of copies made.
  • 文像武像、善威喜怒、壮老仙佛鬼怪 - 文像武像 (civilian and military portraits) refers to different types of portraits, 善威喜怒 (virtue, awe, joy, anger) describes the expressions depicted, 壮老仙佛鬼怪 (young and old, immortals, Buddhas, ghosts, and monsters) lists various subjects of the paintings.
  • 粗工钩勒之不同 - 粗工 (rough work) and 钩勒 (outline) refer to different levels of detail in the paintings.
  • 神奇变幻 - Means 'wondrous and ever-changing,' describing the artist's skill.
  • 显应 - Means 'manifestation' or 'miraculous effect.'
  • 查俭堂 - 查 (Zha) is a surname, 俭堂 (Jiantang) is a place or person's name.
  • 妾发狂如中祟状 - 妾 (concubine) refers to a woman, 发狂 (go mad) and 中祟 (possessed by a spirit) describe her condition.
  • 目中有长髯绿袍大汉 - 目中 (in her eyes) refers to a vision, 长髯绿袍大汉 (a man with long beard and green robe) is the apparition she saw.
  • 惊怖欲绝 - Means 'terrified to the point of death.'
  • 移置画像于床榻被上 - 移置 (move and place), 画像 (portrait), 床榻被上 (on the bed) describe the action.
  • 焚香默祷 - 焚香 (burn incense), 默祷 (silent prayer) describe the ritual.
  • 病辄愈 - 病 (illness), 辄愈 (immediately recover) describe the result.
  • 宁国太守翟宅厅事 - 宁国太守 (Governor of Ningguo), 翟宅 (Zhai's residence), 厅事 (hall) describe the location.
  • 每夜不宁 - 每夜 (every night), 不宁 (restless) describe the condition.
  • 悬公所画像 - 悬 (hang), 公所画像 (the artist's portrait) describe the action.
  • 即静谧 - 即 (then), 静谧 (peaceful) describe the result.
  • 公画龙独开生面 - 公画龙 (the artist's dragon paintings), 独开生面 (unique and innovative) describe the artist's skill.
  • 虔祷雨中 - 虔祷 (sincerely pray), 雨中 (in the rain) describe the context.
  • 得睹真容 - 得睹 (see), 真容 (true form) describe the experience.
  • 画龙有角有耳 - 画龙 (dragon paintings), 有角有耳 (with horns and ears) describe the details.
  • 无碍者 - Means 'without obstruction' or 'unobstructed.'
  • 画虎，头大而胯细 - 画虎 (tiger paintings), 头大而胯细 (large head and slender hips) describe the details.
  • 画工之虎，得其形似，不若吾虎之威也 - 画工之虎 (painter's tigers), 得其形似 (resemble in form), 不若吾虎之威 (do not match the majesty of my tigers) compare the artist's work with others.
  • 谓公乘醉以头画虎者，是齐东语 - 谓 (say), 公乘醉以头画虎 (the artist drawing tigers while drunk), 齐东语 (a fabricated story) indicate a false claim.
  • 画狮不以长毛大尾，似虎非虎，黄色面方，两耳白毫拖地，尾结成球 - 画狮 (lion paintings), 不以长毛大尾 (not with long fur and large tail), 似虎非虎 (resembling a tiger but not a tiger), 黄色面方 (yellow face and square), 两耳白毫拖地 (white hair on both ears reaching the ground), 尾结成球 (tail forming a ball) describe the details.
  • 雍正年间公在御园亲见也 - 雍正年间 (during the Yongzheng era), 公在御园亲见 (the artist personally saw in the imperial garden) provide the context.
  • 山水之兼众妙 - 山水 (landscape paintings), 兼众妙 (combine many wonders) describe the artist's skill.
  • 人物之得真神 - 人物 (figure paintings), 得真神 (capture true spirit) describe the artist's skill.
  • 翎毛花卉，梅柳丛树 - 翎毛 (feathers), 花卉 (flowers and plants), 梅柳丛树 (plum, willow, and grove trees) list the subjects.
  • 或仿古、或没骨、或白描 - 仿古 (imitate ancient styles), 没骨 (boneless technique), 白描 (line drawing) describe the techniques.
  • 莫不各极其精 - 莫不 (all), 各极其精 (each reaching the highest level of skill) describe the artist's mastery.
  • 写照传神，诧为阿堵 - 写照传神 (capture likeness and spirit), 诧为阿堵 (surprising as if it were a treasure) describe the artist's skill.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 钟进士 - A historical figure, likely a scholar-official.
  • 查俭堂 - A place or person's name, possibly a wealthy family or individual.
  • 宁国太守翟宅 - 宁国 (Ningguo) is a place, 太守 (governor), 翟宅 (Zhai's residence) provide the context.
  • 雍正年间 - 雍正 (Yongzheng) is the reign title of the fourth Qing emperor.
  • 御园 - 御园 (imperial garden) refers to a royal garden.

[443]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 公畫自供奉大内 - 公 refers to an artist, 評自供奉 means painted for the imperial court, and 大内 refers to the imperial palace.
  • 以及海内缙绅家，无不索求 - 海内 means throughout the land, 缙绅家 refers to scholars or officials, and 无不索求 means all sought after.
  • 公惟日染指，自壮而老，未尝一刻释手 - 惟日染指 means only engaged in painting daily, 自壮而老 means from youth to old age, and 未尝一刻释手 means never put down the brush for a moment.
  • 约在人间不下数万纸也 - 约在人间 means approximately in the world, and 不下数万纸 means no less than tens of thousands of pieces.
  • 宜乎为一家之冠冕矣 - 宜乎 means it is fitting that, and 一家之冠冕 means the crown of a school or style.
  • 同时如李天涛之指墨焦笔小品 - 同时 means contemporaries, 李天涛 is a person's name, and 指墨焦笔小品 refers to small works done with finger painting and dry brush techniques.
  • 后有朱伦瀚之山水、傅凯亭之人物 - 朱伦瀚 and 傅凯亭 are names, 山水 means landscape, and 人物 means figurative painting.
  • 虽亦各有所长 - 虽亦 means although also, and 各有所长 means each has their strengths.
  • 是皆分公一体，或具体而微者也 - 是皆 means these are all, 分公一体 means part of the same school/style, and 或具体而微者也 means or smaller versions of the same.
  • 孔衍栻，字石村 - 孔衍栻 is a person's name, and 字石村 means style name Shicun.
  • 为稼部公之从子，曲阜人，圣裔也 - 稼部公 is a title, 从子 means nephew, 曲阜人 means from Qufu, and 圣裔 means descendant of Confucius.
  • 贡生，官济宁训导 - 贡生 means tribute scholar, and 官济宁训导 means served as a tutor in Jining.
  • 善画，以渴笔名 - 善画 means skilled in painting, and 渴笔 refers to a dry brush technique.
  • 独辟蹊径 - Means pioneered a new path.
  • 晚年学愈进，寿八十九 - 晚年学愈进 means his studies advanced further in old age, and 寿八十九 means lived to be 89.
  • 自著有《石村画诀》 - 自著有 means wrote himself, and 《石村画诀》 is the title of his book.
  • 古今画家，用水渲染，不易之法也 - 古今画家 means painters throughout history, 用水渲染 means using water to blend colors, and 不易之法也 means an unchanging method.
  • 渴笔烘染，古人未创此境 - 烘染 means blending, and 古人未创此境 means ancient people did not create this realm.
  • 余幼师石田 - 余幼 means in my youth, and 师石田 means studied under Shitai.

  • 乃穷日夜之思，忽结别想 - 乃穷日夜之思 means then exhausted days and nights thinking, and 忽结别想 means suddenly came up with a different idea.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 立意 - Intention or concept behind the painting.
  • 取神 - Capture the essence or spirit of the subject.
  • 运笔 - Technique of brushwork.
  • 造景 - Create or compose the scenery.
  • 位置 - Arrangement or placement of elements.
  • 避俗 - Avoid clichés or commonplaces.
  • 点缀 - Use of small details to enhance the composition.
  • 渴染 - Specific painting technique involving dry brushwork.
  • 款识 - Signature or inscription on the painting.
  • 图章 - Seal or stamp used in the painting.
  • 墨少着水，重磨 - Process of grinding ink with less water to make it thicker.
  • 用秃湖颖，不着水，即蘸焦墨 - Using a worn brush without water and dipping it in concentrated ink.
  • 先用别纸试，微润，轻拂画上 - Testing the brush on a separate piece of paper to achieve a light, moist effect before applying it to the painting.
  • 笔笔匀，可染二三次 - Evenly applying the brushstroke, which can be done two or three times.
  • 惟无笔痕为妙，颇有秀色 - Best result is when no brushstrokes are visible, giving a refined appearance.
  • 凡五叶树，俱用渴笔实染双钩，叶白者不染 - Using dry brushwork to outline and shade leaves, leaving white leaves untouched.
  • 房舍有瓦草处染，无瓦草处空白 - Shading the parts of houses that have tiles or grass, leaving the rest blank.
  • 室内人物器具空白，周围俱用渴笔剔清 - Leaving the interior of rooms and objects blank and using dry brushwork to define the edges.
  • 每一石止渴染皴处，石顶空白，石根宜用重染 - Using dry brushwork on the texture of rocks, leaving the top blank and heavily shading the base.
  • 大山平坡皆然 - Applying the same technique to large mountains and flat slopes.
  • 远山先用炭为轮廓，外用渴染，天气渐与之接 - Outlining distant mountains with charcoal and then using dry brushwork to blend them into the background.
  • 远山空白，山根用渴染 - Leaving distant mountains blank and shading their bases.
  • 波水溪江，俱用平直笔密画出，有聚有散，俱用渴染托出 - Using straight, dense strokes to depict water bodies, with clusters and spreads, and using dry brushwork to define their edges.
  • 云烟断续，须轻染，渐渐不见乃妙 - Lightly shading clouds and mist so that they gradually fade away, creating a natural effect.
  • 非有定，惟画者自裁 - No fixed rule, the artist must decide based on their own judgment.
  • 有墨画处，此实笔也；无墨画处，以云气衬，此虚中之实也 - Areas with ink as solid brushwork and areas without ink as virtual space filled with cloud and mist, which is a form of solid within the void.
  • 树石房廊等，皆有白处，又实中之虚也 - Trees, rocks, and buildings all have blank areas, which represent the void within the solid.
  • 实者虚之，虚者实之 - Balancing solid and void elements in the painting.
  • 满幅皆笔迹到处，却又不见笔痕，但觉一片灵气浮动于其上 - Entire painting covered with brushstrokes, yet no brushstrokes are visible, creating a sense of ethereal energy.

  • 罘罳 - A fushī is a lattice or screen, often used in ancient Chinese architecture to separate spaces.
  • 《白头吟》 - A classical Chinese poem, traditionally attributed to Ban Jieyu (Ban Ji), a concubine of Emperor Cheng of the Western Han Dynasty. It is a lament about unfulfilled love and fidelity.

  • 今田园自力，梁间事绝口不谈 - Contrast between the current peaceful life and past criminal activities.
  • 我年七十有三，已事耳，亦足以佐一觞乎？ - Emphasizes the speaker's age and sense of completion, 'my life is nearly over.'
  • 参横夜半，客请执折叉，御户以安 - Refers to the position of the Big Dipper at midnight, indicating the time and the speaker's actions.

  • 小骨子 - A colloquial term for 'bones,' often used in a derogatory or humorous context.
  • 炊饼 - Steamed cakes, a common food item in ancient China.
  • 豪气 - A term meaning 'boldness' or 'courage,' often used to describe a person's character.

  • 忽龍騰身 - 'Suddenly a dragon rose up.' A dramatic and mythological expression.
  • 擲民於岸上 - 'Threw the man onto the shore.' A verb phrase indicating a miraculous or supernatural action.
  • 民以思母得生 - 'The man was saved due to his longing for his mother.' A moral or miraculous cause-effect relationship.
  • 誠能動物 - 'Indeed, it can move animals.' The term '動物' here is likely used in a philosophical or moral sense, not the modern biological term.
  • 理固有之 - 'This is indeed a natural principle.' A classical expression of affirming a moral or natural law.
  • 為道士李常明所建 - A construction phrase used in classical Chinese.
  • 其或有驅靈伏怪之術也欤？ - A rhetorical question with a speculative tone.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 大沽橋 - A real historical bridge in Jimo, Shandong Province. Its name may have symbolic or historical significance.
  • 即墨 - A city in Shandong Province, historically significant in ancient China.
  • 道士李常明 - A historical or fictional Daoist figure, likely a local religious or spiritual leader.
  • 驅靈伏怪 - A reference to Daoist or folk religious practices, common in classical Chinese literature.
  • 南山獵 - A narrative title or section title, possibly indicating a story or account from the South Mountain region.
  • 東撫某公 - A reference to a local official or governor in the eastern region. The term '撫' likely refers to a governor or administrator.
  • 卜某日畋於岱山之陰 - A reference to Mount Dai (Dai Mountain), a historically and culturally significant mountain in Shandong, associated with the ancient state of Qi.
  • 講武事 - A phrase used to justify hunting as a form of military training or preparation.

  • 五色髯 - A fantastical or supernatural description, likely of spirits or demons.
  • 三目鬼面 - Another supernatural description, indicating non-human or demonic entities.
  • 長喙鳩形 - A description of a bird-like or avian-shaped spirit or demon.
  • 小鉦 - A musical or ritual instrument used in spiritual or ceremonial contexts.
  • 虎狼狐兔，雉雁雀鹞 - A list of animals, likely representing a variety of beasts and birds that were summoned or gathered.
  • 按簿而呼 - A reference to a supernatural ledger or record of souls or creatures.
  • 群隊俯應 - A ritualistic or supernatural response to the summons.
  • 白狐 - A symbol of intelligence, transformation, or supernatural power in Chinese folklore.
  • 撫軍某公 - A high-ranking official, possibly the same as the earlier '東撫某公,' indicating the same person.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 我但宣示刑名 - Formal expression meaning 'I merely announce the names of punishments,' indicating a formal or legal context.

  • 吾不与闻 - Phrase meaning 'I do not interfere' or 'I do not get involved,' indicating a neutral stance.

  • 而使者且为之前驱 - Phrase meaning 'and the messenger will lead the way,' suggesting a role in the pursuit.

  • 殆六十甲子矣 - Phrase meaning 'it is almost sixty years,' indicating a long time.
  • 当助为力，成兹无量功德 - Statement meaning 'I will assist and help to complete this great merit,' indicating a commitment to a moral or religious cause.
  • 众兽忻舞，各有喜色 - Phrase meaning 'the beasts were delighted and each had a joyful expression,' indicating a positive reaction.
  • 髯者执炬皆出 - Phrase meaning 'the bearded one held a torch and they all went out,' indicating a physical action.

  • 惟古柏间鸡啼啁啁 - Phrase meaning 'only the old cypress trees had the sound of chickens chirping,' indicating a peaceful scene.
  • 翌朝，畋事果寝 - Clause meaning 'the next morning, the hunting affair was indeed abandoned,' indicating a resolution.
  • 又相传开府瘫，不出视事矣 - Statement meaning 'it was also said that the governor-general was paralyzed and did not leave his office,' indicating a physical or mental state.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 抚军 - Refers to a military governor, a high-ranking official in charge of military affairs.
  • 鹰鹯之逐 - Metaphor for a fierce pursuit, often used to describe a hunting or military campaign.
  • 使者 - Refers to a messenger or envoy, often sent to deliver important messages or perform tasks.
  • 青州 - One of the nine provinces of ancient China, located in the eastern part of the country.
  • 六十甲子 - Traditional Chinese calendar system that cycles every 60 years, often used to denote a long period of time.
  • 苦露 - Type of wine, often associated with medicinal or ceremonial use.
  • 开府 - High-ranking official, often a governor-general or a similar position.
  • 视事 - Term meaning 'to handle official duties' or 'to be in office.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • Long sentence must be read as a series of conditional statements, each addressing a different aspect of the situation.
  • Clause links the military governor's actions to his loss of public support and divine favor.
  • Clause criticizes the military governor's behavior and the role of the messenger.
  • Statement indicates a lack of power or influence.
  • Clause describes a plan to seduce someone using a specific method.
  • Clause introduces a mythical or symbolic figure and their commitment to a moral or religious cause.
  • Phrase describes the positive reaction of the beasts.
  • Phrase describes a physical action.
  • Phrase describes a change in the environment and a peaceful scene.
  • Clause describes the resolution of the hunting affair and the state of the governor-general.

Proper Nouns

  • 抚军 - The military governor.
  • 使者 - The messenger or envoy.
  • 青州 - The province of Qingzhou.
  • 开府 - The governor-general.
  • 视事 - Handling official duties.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 可為耽酒渔色者戒 - Classical moral warning meaning 'may serve as a warning to those who indulge in wine and women.'
  • 耽酒渔色 - Compound expression meaning 'to indulge in wine and women,' used to warn against moral laxity.
  • 瞽者搏虎 - Title or heading meaning 'The Blind Man Fights a Tiger,' a metaphor or anecdote conveying a moral or cautionary tale.
  • 曳杖獨行郊外 - 'Drawing his staff, he walked alone in the suburbs.' **曳杖** is a classical way of describing an elderly or blind person walking with a staff.
  • 誤以為人之擊己也 - 'Mistook it for someone striking him.' Reflects a misunderstanding or misperception common in Classical Chinese narrative.
  • 相與奔 - 'They ran together.' Shows mutual action between the blind man and the tiger.
  • 眢井 - 'A dry well.' A specific term describing an abandoned or dry well, an important setting in the story.
  • 扶瞽起于甃 - 'Helped the blind man up from the well’s edge.' **甃** refers to the stone or brick lining of a well, often used in classical texts.
  • 納諸穽 - 'Put into the trap.' **穽** is a trap or pit, often used in the context of hunting or capturing animals.
  • 身扼毙 - 'Died by being strangled.' **扼** means to choke or strangle, and **毙** means to die.
  • 善士也 - 'A virtuous man.' A classical way of praising someone’s moral character.
  • 集訊多人 - 'Assembled many people for interrogation.' A legal or judicial term suggesting a formal trial or hearing.
  • 一鳥翔下，衔其首辞片纸而去 - 'A bird flew down, took a piece of paper from his head, and flew away.' **衔其首辞片纸** is a poetic and metaphorical expression; the bird takes a document or evidence from the person's head, symbolizing justice or divine retribution.
  • 雪冤苦 - 'To clear the冤 (wrong) and relieve the苦 (suffering).' A phrase used in legal and moral contexts, meaning to exonerate someone from false accusations.
  • 哀鳴眾口皆呶呶 - 'The mournful cries of many mouths all clamor.' A poetic expression suggesting the collective voice of the people in protest or lamentation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 瞽者 - A blind person. In classical Chinese, **瞽** is often used to refer to blind individuals, and sometimes metaphorically to those who lack insight or wisdom.
  • 義鳥亭 - 'The Pavilion of the Righteous Bird.' A proper name of a structure, likely built in memory of the event described in the second story.
  • 宜興 - A county in Jiangsu province, historically known for its cultural and literary significance.
  • 毘陵地方城內 - 'Within the city of Piling.' **毘陵** is an ancient name for the area around modern-day Changzhou, Jiangsu province.
  • 好事者有《禽言》五首 - 'Those with interest composed five poems titled 'The Words of Birds.' Refers to a poetic collection, likely composed in response to the miraculous event described in the story.
  • 鹁鸪鸪 - A type of bird, commonly known as the **bubbling gu**, whose call is said to resemble its name. In Chinese folklore, birds often symbolize justice, omens, or moral guidance.
  • 南山有羊我不食，东村晒谷我不图 - 'I do not eat the sheep of the南山 (south mountain), nor do I take the rice of the east village.' A symbolic refrain in the bird’s song, suggesting the bird’s moral integrity and non-interference in human affairs.
  • 替我恩人雪冤苦 - 'To clear the冤 (wrong) and relieve the苦 (suffering) of my benefactor.' A moral and poetic expression of justice and retribution.

Proper Nouns

  • 諸城 - A county in Shandong province, likely the location of the first story.
  • 宜興 - A county in Jiangsu province, the location of the second story.
  • 毘陵地方城內 - A location referring to the city of Piling (modern Changzhou), Jiangsu province.
  • 《禽言》五首 - A title of a poetic collection, likely composed by an unknown author in response to the story.

Idiomatic Expressions

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 清婉合律 - Means 'clear and graceful, in harmony with the rules,' describing the quality of the parrot's singing.
  • 跨蹇驴出游 - Means 'riding a slow donkey on outings,' referring to the leisurely nature of the trips.
  • 强纳百金以买 - Means 'forcibly bought for a hundred gold pieces,' indicating the high price paid for the parrot.

  • 歌哭尽日乃去 - Means 'singing and crying all day before leaving,' describing the emotional state of the student.
  • 按歌犹带雪衣娘 - A poetic expression meaning 'still carrying the memory of Snowy Girl while singing,' referring to the parrot's singing evoking memories.
  • 人间何事足欢场 - Means 'What in the world is worth celebrating?' expressing a sense of disillusionment.

  • 金谷无缘惠好音 - Means 'No chance to hear good sounds in Jin Gu,' referring to the inability to enjoy the parrot's singing in a specific place.
  • 羞向华筵唱渭城 - Means 'Ashamed to sing 'The Song of the Wei River' at a grand banquet,' expressing a sense of embarrassment or reluctance.
  • 相思一夕顿捐生 - Means 'Missing someone so much that one gives up life in a single night,' describing intense longing leading to despair.
  • 吟魂莫恋知音者 - Means 'The soul of the poet should not cling to a true appreciator,' advising against becoming overly attached to a connoisseur.
  • 安否难传陇上声 - Means 'It's hard to convey news of safety from the western border,' referring to the difficulty of communicating from distant places.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 金蚕蛊 - A term referring to a type of toxic insect or spirit used in Chinese folk magic, specifically a 'gold silkworm' 蛊. The term '金蚕' (golden silkworm) is metaphorical, not referring to an actual insect.
  • 养蛊家 - A person or family who practices the art of raising or cultivating 蛊 (a form of toxic spirit or poison used in traditional Chinese magic).
  • 杀人渔利 - Literally 'kill people to gain profit.' A phrase describing the practice of using 蛊 to harm others for financial gain.
  • 利得亦自杀 - 'Once the profit is obtained, they also commit suicide.' A common fate for those who use 蛊, implying that the practice is cursed or brings retribution.
  • 端午日 - The fifth day of the fifth lunar month, a traditional Chinese festival, also known as Dragon Boat Festival, often associated with rituals to ward off evil spirits.
  • 蛇蝎蟆诸毒物 - A list of venomous creatures: snakes, scorpions, toads, and other toxic animals.
  • 听其自咬 - Let them bite each other. A ritual practice in 蛊-making.
  • 将尽死，独一物生 - When nearly all are dead, only one remains alive. This is the 蛊 spirit, the most potent.
  • 以时饲之 - Feed it regularly.
  • 雏匹三年 - Raise it for three years. '雏匹' may be a variant or misspelling of '雏匹' (young offspring), but in this context, it refers to the time needed to raise the 蛊.
  • 杂以五色绫锦 - Mix in colorful silk fabrics. A ritual offering to the 蛊.
  • 裂而饵之 - Tear the fabric and feed it to the 蛊.
  • 最灵 - Most powerful or sensitive.
  • 奉之者凡一动一作，皆尊承而不敢稍狎于心 - Anyone who respects it will be cautious in their every action and movement, lest they incur misfortune.
  • 暂将日变月化，形遂隐 - It can transform from day to month, and its form becomes invisible.
  • 俾其行毒 - Let it go out and spread poison.
  • 试一人 - Test it on one person.
  • 无过客，则以家人当之 - If no stranger is available, it will target family members.
  • 中毒，绞毒吐逆，十指如墨，嚼豆不腥，含矾不涩 - Symptoms of 蛊 poisoning: convulsions, vomiting, blackened fingers, no smell when chewing beans, no bitterness when holding alum.
  • 是其验也 - This is the test or verification.
  • 祈求粮米银钱，无不如意 - Pray for grain, money, and silver, and all will be granted.
  • 按月必蛊一人以为飨蛊者 - Every month, it must poison one person to feed the 蛊.
  • 盖以其粪纳饮食中云 - Because it is believed that the 蛊 is fed by putting its excrement into food and drink.
  • 赤贫 - Extremely poor, destitute.
  • 无以为生 - No means of livelihood.
  • 蓄一蛊 - Raise or cultivate one 蛊.
  • 家巨富 - Became very wealthy.
  • 厮仆 - Servants or laborers.
  • 夭死 - Die young or suddenly.
  • 门致可罗雀 - The gate is so desolate that sparrows can be caught there. A metaphor for extreme poverty or abandonment.
  • 设酒肆于通衢 - Set up a wine shop on a main road or thoroughfare.
  • 渐亦有侦察之者 - Gradually, some people began to investigate or suspect.
  • 解貂人虽过门不入也 - Those who wear furs (wealthy people) pass by the door but do not enter.
  • 索食甚急 - The 蛊 is demanding food very urgently.
  • 求之去而不得矣 - Tried to get rid of it but could not.
  • 荷珠已适人 - The eldest daughter, He Zhu, has already married.
  • 年皆及瓜 - All have reached marriageable age (the 'age of the melon,' a traditional term for when a woman is eligible to marry).
  • 咸以蛊故，乡里评旦焉 - All because of the 蛊, the village people criticized them at dawn.
  • 遂一妁不至 - So not a single matchmaker came.
  • 后年馀，其大倩死蛊也 - After a year or more, the eldest daughter died of the 蛊.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 蛊 - A traditional Chinese concept of a harmful spirit or poison, often associated with witchcraft and folk magic. It is believed to be created by mixing various poisons and left to fight among themselves, with the survivor becoming the 蛊 spirit.
  • 端午日 - The Dragon Boat Festival, traditionally associated with rituals to ward off evil and disease. It is also a time when 蛊-making is said to take place.
  • 蛇蝎蟆诸毒物 - References to venomous animals, which are commonly used in 蛊-making.
  • 五色绫锦 - Five-colored silk fabrics, used in ritual offerings or to feed the 蛊.
  • 赤贫 - A term from classical Chinese indicating extreme poverty, often used to describe those who are destitute and have no resources.
  • 通衢 - A main road or thoroughfare, often associated with commercial or public activity.
  • 解貂人 - A reference to wealthy individuals who wear furs (貂 refers to sable or other luxurious furs). This is a metaphor for the rich who avoid the family due to suspicion of 蛊.
  • 及瓜 - A traditional term for when a woman reaches marriageable age, derived from the idea that a melon is ripe when it is about to be cut, symbolizing readiness for marriage.
  • 乡里评旦 - A phrase indicating that the village people publicly criticized the family at dawn, a form of social condemnation in traditional Chinese society.
  • 大倩 - A term for the eldest daughter, likely a variant or honorific form of '大女' (eldest daughter). It may also be a name, though not clearly identified in the text.

Proper Nouns

  • 金蚕蛊 - A compound term referring to a type of 蛊; no direct translation or substitution is possible; must be rendered as is or with a descriptive explanation.
  • 滇中 - A geographical term referring to the region of Yunnan Province in southwest China.
  • 宜良 - A place name, likely referring to Yiliang County in Yunnan Province.
  • 章姓夫妇 - A couple from the Zhang family. The surname 章 is preserved as is.
  • 荷珠 - The name of the eldest daughter, likely a personal name.

'鹧鸪' (partridge) might be a metaphor or a personal emblem. '二十年前舊板橋' (an old bridge from twenty years ago) could be a nostalgic reference.
  • 皆別致，大半吾鄉朱文震所刻 - 'All are unique, most of them carved by Zhu Wen Zhen from my hometown'; '別致' (unique) emphasizes the distinctiveness of the seals. '吾鄉' (my hometown) and '朱文震' (Zhu Wen Zhen) are specific references to the place and the person who carved the seals.
  • 其詩钞、詞钞、家書、小唱，皆手自書之 - 'His poetry collections, song collections, family letters, and short songs, all written by hand'; '手自書之' (written by hand) emphasizes the personal touch and authenticity.
  • 其門人司徒文膏镂板亦精 - 'His disciple Simu Wengao's engraved boards are also exquisite'; '镂板' (engraved boards) refers to the printing blocks used for reproducing texts.
  • 又附“道情”數阕於左 - 'Also attached below are several pieces of 'Daoqing' (religious songs); '道情' (Daoqing) is a form of religious or moral poetry.
  • 老渔翁，一钓竿，靠山厓，傍水湾，扁舟来往无牵绊 - 'An old fisherman, with one fishing rod, leaning against the mountain cliff, beside the water bend, a small boat coming and going without any hindrance'; this is a descriptive verse that paints a picture of a simple, free life.
  • 沙鸥点点轻波远，荻港萧萧白昼寒，高歌一曲斜阳晚 - 'Sandpipers dot the light waves far away, the reed port is desolate in the cold daylight, singing a song at dusk'; this continues the imagery of the fisherman's life.
  • 一霎时波翻金影，蓦抬头月上东山 - 'In a moment, the waves turn golden, suddenly looking up, the moon rises over the eastern mountain'; this describes a sudden change in the scene.
  • 老樵夫，自砍柴，捆青松，夹绿槐，茫茫野草秋山外 - 'An old woodcutter, cutting his own firewood, binding green pines, carrying green locusts, the vast wild grass beyond the autumn mountains'; this describes a woodcutter's life.
  • 丰碑是处成荒冢，华表千寻卧碧苔，坟前石马磨刀坏 - 'Monuments everywhere become deserted graves, the tall pillars lie covered in green moss, the stone horses in front of the tomb are worn down by the grindstone'; this describes the decay of grand monuments.
  • 倒不如闲钱沽酒，醉醺醺山径归来 - 'It's better to spend idle money on wine, returning home drunk along the mountain path'; this expresses a preference for a simple, carefree life.
  • 老头陀，古庙中，自烧香，自打钟，兔葵燕麦闲斋供 - 'An old monk, in an ancient temple, burning incense, ringing the bell, the rabbit and sparrow grass serving as offerings'; this describes a monk's life.
  • 山门破落无关锁，斜日苍黄有乱松，秋星闪烁颓垣缝 - 'The temple gate is dilapidated, no lock or bolt, the setting sun casts a pale light on the tangled pines, autumn stars flicker through the cracks in the ruined walls'; this continues the imagery of the temple.
  • 黑漆漆蒲团打坐，夜烧茶炉火通红 - 'Darkly, sitting on a meditation cushion, the night tea stove's fire burns bright red'; this describes the monk's meditation.

  • 修琴卖药般般会，捉鬼拿妖件件能 - 'Skilled in repairing musical instruments and selling medicine, capable of catching ghosts and demons'; this highlights the Taoist's skills.

  • 陽奉而陰違 - Means 'paying lip service but acting in secret against' or 'obeying outwardly but disobeying inwardly.'

  • 亢陽 - Refers to a prolonged period of drought.

  • 何庸以一请为荣耶 - Means 'why should one consider a single invitation as an honor?'

  • 窃惟 - A humble way of expressing a personal thought or opinion, often used in formal or official contexts.
  • 官以治明，神以理幽 - A contrast between the duties of officials in the human world and the role of deities in the spiritual realm.
  • 不职而殃民则罚随 - If officials fail in their duties and harm the people, they will face punishment.
  • 不灵而灾民则祀绝 - If deities fail to protect the people and cause disasters, they will lose their worship.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 及其归，以银计息，布称是 - Introduces a financial system: when he returns, he calculates interest in silver, and the amount of cloth is equivalent.

  • 日会而月计之 - Daily meetings and monthly accounting; a structured system of oversight and management.
  • 籴贱粜贵 - Classical economic strategy: buying low and selling high.
  • 权子母 - Term from classical Chinese finance, referring to the manipulation of capital (子 = profit, 母 = principal).

  • 得此以益，家愈饶 - By obtaining this, his family grew even wealthier; a common expression of increasing prosperity.
  • 惟其财之裕也，故其心之悭 - Because his wealth was abundant, his heart was stingy; a contrast between wealth and frugality or greed.
  • 余辛丑过赣 - In the year Xin Chou, I passed through Gan; indicating a specific historical time and location.
  • 作竟夜抵足之谈 - We had a conversation that lasted all night, with our feet touching; a poetic way of expressing deep, extended conversation.
  • 餐用饲狸小鱼、马齿苋菜 - For meals, we ate small fish to feed the cat and ma chi xian vegetables; a humorous or ironic description of meager food.
  • 若只鸡、豚蹄，固未之前闻也 - If only a chicken or pig’s trotter, it was certainly something I had never heard of before; expressing astonishment at the scarcity of food.
  • 师疾卒于官 - The teacher died of illness in office; a classical way of stating that someone died while in service.
  • 家口甚繁指 - The family was very large and numerous; indicating the complexity of the household.
  • 太师母与师母及眷属扶榇归里百十人 - The mother of the grand teacher, the teacher's wife, and their relatives, a hundred or so people, carried the coffin back to their hometown; a formal description of a large mourning procession.
  • 内外数十席 - Dozens of tables inside and outside; indicating a large banquet or gathering.
  • 碗箸匙不计数 - Bowls, chopsticks, and spoons were not counted; suggesting abundance or excess.
  • 即晚百人需百床，皆取诸宫中，不缺一 - Even in the evening, a hundred people needed a hundred beds, all taken from the palace, not one missing; indicating the vast resources of the palace.
  • 既观其仓有余谷，箧有余布，园圃多蔬菜，池塘鱼鳖不可胜食 - Having observed that the granaries had surplus grain, the chests had surplus cloth, the garden had many vegetables, and the ponds had an endless supply of fish and turtles; a detailed description of material abundance.
  • 若吾师宦囊中携归之端溪砚田，不可耕而耨也 - As for the Duanxi inkstone fields that my teacher brought back from his official career, they cannot be tilled or weeded; a metaphor for unproductive wealth.
  • 英德美石，不可煮而食也 - The beautiful stones of Yingde cannot be boiled and eaten; another metaphor for unproductive or impractical wealth.
  • 书册画卷，不可寒而衣也 - The books and scrolls cannot be worn as clothing to keep out the cold; another metaphor for useless wealth.
  • 相与束之高阁 - Together, we placed them on the high shelf; a common expression for neglecting or storing away something unused.
  • 夫然后一家之人，皆食大郎之食，衣大郎之衣 - Only then did the entire family eat from the food of the eldest son and wear the clothes of the eldest son; indicating that the eldest son provided for the whole family.
  • 迄于今又十年，恒取给焉，无所匮 - Up to now, another ten years have passed, and they have always been provided for, with no shortages; a statement of continued provision and stability.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 辛丑 - Refers to the Xin Chou year in the Chinese sexagenary cycle; corresponds to a specific year in the traditional Chinese calendar.
  • 赣 - Refers to Gan (modern-day Ganzhou, Jiangxi province); a region in southern China.
  • 端溪砚田 - Duanxi is a famous inkstone producing area in Guangdong province; the term 砚田 is a metaphor for 'inkstone fields,' meaning a source of writing or literary wealth.
  • 英德 - A county in Guangdong province known for its beautiful stones, particularly Yingde stones used in carving and decoration.
  • 书册画卷 - Refers to books, scrolls, and paintings, which were highly valued in traditional Chinese culture, especially among scholars.
  • 太师母与师母 - Taishi mu is the wife of the Taishi (Grand Tutor), a high-ranking official in the imperial court; shi mu is the wife of the teacher, indicating a family hierarchy.
  • 扶榇归里 - Refers to the act of carrying the coffin of a deceased person back to their hometown for burial; a traditional and solemn custom in Chinese culture.
  • 宫中 - Refers to the palace or imperial residence; indicating a large and well-resourced household.
  • 束之高阁 - A well-known idiom meaning to store something away and not use it; often used to describe unused knowledge or wealth.
  • 大郎 - A term of address for the eldest son; commonly used in traditional Chinese families.

Proper Nouns

  • 硕夫 - A personal name, likely Shuo Fu, the subject of the passage.
  • 赣 - The name of a region, modern-day Ganzhou, Jiangxi province.
  • 端溪 - A famous inkstone producing area in Guangdong province.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 如是二年有余 - Means 'for two years or more,' indicating a duration of time.
  • 无事时柳犹咿唔章句于梦魂鸡火间也 - 'When there were no events, Liu would recite verses in his dreams and between dawn and dusk.' '咿唔' (yi2wu2) is an onomatopoeic term for reading or reciting aloud, and '梦魂鸡火' (meng4hun2ji1huo3) refers to the time between dawn and dusk, symbolizing the passage of time in a poetic way.
  • 岁次戊申乡比 - 'In the year Wu Shen, the local examination.' '岁次' (sui4ci4) means 'in the year of' and is followed by the stem-branch year designation '戊申' (wu3shen1), which corresponds to a specific year in the Chinese calendar.
  • 柳诡言于王曰 - 'Liu deceitfully told Wang.' '诡言' (gui3yan2) means 'to lie' or 'to deceive.'
  • 弟欲还乡一省家门 - 'I want to return home to visit my family.' '弟' (di4) is a humble term for 'I' or 'myself.'
  • 往返约可月余耳 - 'The round trip will take about a month.' '约可' (yue4ke3) means 'about' or 'approximately.'
  • 王即为之治装 - 'Wang immediately prepared his luggage.' '治装' (zhi4zhuang1) means 'to prepare one's belongings.'
  • 衣履悉更 - 'He changed his clothes and shoes.' '悉更' (xi1geng1) means 'all changed' or 'completely changed.'
  • 复厚赠之 - 'And gave him generous gifts.' '复' (fu4) means 'again' or 'furthermore,' and '厚赠' (hou4zeng4) means 'generous gifts.'
  • 柳别王就道 - 'Liu bade farewell to Wang and set off on the road.' '别' (bie4) means 'to bid farewell,' and '就道' (jiu4dao4) means 'to set off on the road.'
  • 易东辙而南辕 - 'He changed from traveling east to traveling south.' '易' (yi4) means 'to change,' '东辙' (dong1zhe2) means 'eastern path,' and '南辕' (nan2yuan2) means 'southern destination.'
  • 至省录遗 - 'He reached the provincial examination.' '省录' (sheng3lu4) means 'provincial examination.'
  • 场事终返 - 'After the examination was over, he returned.' '场事' (chang2shi4) means 'examination,' and '终返' (zhong1fan3) means 'finally returned.'
  • 王以其自青州来也 - 'Wang thought he had come from Qingzhou.' '以其' (yi3qi2) means 'thinking that,' and '自青州来' (zi4qing1zhou1lai2) means 'came from Qingzhou.'
  • 时将重九 - 'It was close to the Double Ninth Festival.' '重九' (chong2jiu3) is another name for the Double Ninth Festival, celebrated on the ninth day of the ninth lunar month.
  • 东省揭晓多在三、四两日 - 'The results of the eastern provincial examination were usually announced on the third or fourth day.' '揭晓' (jiao1xi1) means 'to announce the results.'
  • 柳屈指，心怦怦动 - 'Liu counted the days, and his heart beat rapidly.' '屈指' (qu2zhi3) means 'to count on one's fingers,' and '怦怦动' (peng1peng1dong4) describes a rapid heartbeat.
  • 是年新城落科 - 'That year, Xincheng failed the examination.' '落科' (luo4ke1) means 'to fail the examination.'
  • 故无耗 - 'So there was no news.' '无耗' (wu2hao4) means 'no news' or 'no result.'
  • 越日，闻传榜首出寿光 - 'The next day, he heard that the top scorer came from Shouguang.' '越日' (yue4ri4) means 'the next day,' '闻传' (wen2chuan2) means 'heard that,' and '榜首' (pang3shou3) means 'top scorer.'
  • 柳不怿 - 'Liu was unhappy.' '不怿' (bu4yi4) means 'unhappy' or 'displeased.'
  • 出村口蹀躞于大槐荫间 - 'He walked back and forth at the village entrance under the shade of a large locust tree.' '蹀躞' (die4xie2) means 'to walk back and forth,' and '大槐荫' (da4huai2yin1) means 'under the shade of a large locust tree.'
  • 遥见两人喘而来 - 'He saw two people panting as they approached.' '遥见' (yao2jian4) means 'saw from afar,' and '喘而来' (chuan4er4lai2) means 'panting as they approached.'
  • 坐树根 - 'They sat at the base of the tree.' '树根' (shu4gen1) means 'base of the tree.'
  • 柳视之，似传报者 - 'Liu looked at them and thought they were messengers.' '视之' (shi4zhi1) means 'looked at them,' and '传报者' (chuan2bao4zhe2) means 'messengers.'
  • 柳心痒 - 'Liu's heart itched.' '心痒' (xin1yang3) is a metaphor for being eager or impatient.
  • 问曰 - 'He asked.' '问曰' (wen4yue4) means 'asked.'
  • 二位何往 - 'Where are you going?' '何往' (he2wang3) means 'where are you going?'
