  • 奚奴火扇初红 - The fire fan (a device used to stoke the fire) is just beginning to glow red, indicating the fire is being lit.
  • 蟹眼翻花际也 - The water is starting to bubble like a crab's eyes, a common metaphor for the beginning of boiling.
  • 蒋怅怅若失 - Jiang feels a sense of loss or emptiness, suggesting a feeling of dissatisfaction or longing.
  • 两腋风已胜于七碗后矣 - The cool breeze in Jiang's armpits is more refreshing than the feeling after drinking seven cups of tea, a reference to the famous poem 'Drinking Alone by Moonlight' by Li Bai.
  • 四十年如炊黍 - Forty years have passed like the time it takes to cook millet, a metaphor for the passage of time.
  • 贵不如贱，富不如贫 - Wealth and status are not as important as poverty and lowly status, reflecting a philosophical view on material wealth and social standing.
  • 利欲萦怀 - Greed and desire dominate one's thoughts, indicating a preoccupation with material gain.
  • 危机祸水 - A source of danger and disaster, a metaphor for the negative consequences of greed and desire.
  • 逍遥苕、霅 - To live a carefree life in the regions of Taoshan and Zhazhi, referring to a peaceful and idyllic lifestyle.
  • 武陵源可指迷津 - The source of the Wuling River can guide one to the right path, a metaphor for finding guidance and direction.
  • 古人濠濮间 - The ancient people who lived near the Hao and Pu rivers, a reference to a place of tranquility and natural beauty.
  • 会心不在远 - True understanding and appreciation do not depend on physical proximity, emphasizing the importance of inner harmony and contentment.
  • 一年风宪 - One year in office as an official, indicating a brief but significant period of service.
  • 告归，作林下翁 - Resigning from office and becoming a hermit in the woods, a metaphor for retiring from public life.
  • 小黄粱 - A dream of a small millet, a reference to the story of the 'Yellow Millet Dream' (黄粱梦), where a man dreams of a long life of wealth and power only to wake up and find it was all a dream.
  • 吕公子 - A nobleman's son, indicating a person of high social status.
  • 宫保 - A high-ranking official, often a title given to a military governor or a powerful minister.
  • 家财盈溪壑 - His family's wealth is as abundant as the rivers and valleys, indicating extreme wealth.
  • 高明之家，鬼瞰其室 - A wealthy and prominent family is watched by ghosts, a metaphor for the constant threat of envy and danger.
  • 轻财好施 - Generous and willing to give away wealth, indicating a noble and charitable disposition.
  • 小春申 - A nickname for the nobleman's son, possibly referring to the historical figure Chunshen Jun (春申君), a famous chancellor of the state of Chu during the Warring States period.
  • 速贫为愈 - Embracing poverty quickly is better, reflecting a philosophical view on the value of simplicity and detachment from material wealth.
  • 食客颇多 - Many guests or followers, indicating a large entourage or retinue.
  • 方丈宴饮，动费万钱 - Large banquets that cost a lot of money, emphasizing the opulence and extravagance of the nobleman's lifestyle.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 四十年如炊黍 - A metaphor for the passage of time, often used to express the fleeting nature of life.
  • 七碗后 - A reference to the famous poem 'Drinking Alone by Moonlight' by Li Bai, where the poet describes the refreshing feeling after drinking seven cups of tea.
  • 宫保 - A high-ranking official, often a title given to a military governor or a powerful minister, indicating a position of significant power and influence.
  • 高明之家，鬼瞰其室 - A metaphor from the *Book of Han* (汉书) that warns against the dangers of wealth and fame, suggesting that the more one has, the more one is envied and threatened.
  • 小春申 - A reference to Chunshen Jun (春申君), a famous chancellor of the state of Chu during the Warring States period, known for his wisdom and generosity.
  • 食客颇多 - The practice of having many guests or followers, a common feature of noble households in ancient China, reflecting the social and political networks of the time.
  • 方丈宴饮，动费万钱 - The opulence and extravagance of large banquets, a reflection of the wealth and status of the nobleman and the social customs of the time.

Proper Nouns

  • 蒋 - Jiang, the main character of the story.
  • 奚奴 - A servant or attendant.
  • 宫保 - A high-ranking official, often a title given to a military governor or a powerful minister.
  • 小春申 - A nickname for the nobleman's son, possibly referring to the historical figure Chunshen Jun.
  • 吕公子 - A nobleman's son, the main subject of the second part of the text.

[556]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 有客善吹 - A guest skilled at playing (a musical instrument).
  • 席间忽坠其羊脂玉笛 - During the banquet, the guest accidentally dropped his jade flute.
  • 吕曰：“久不闻此碎玉声。” - Lu said, 'I haven't heard this sound of broken jade for a long time.'
  • 遂相与纵谈如故 - They then continued their conversation as before.
  • 园中海棠将开 - In the garden, the crabapple flowers are about to bloom.
  • 吕颦眉独倚亚栏 - Lu frowned and stood alone leaning against a railing.
  • 春愁没可奈何 - Spring sorrow, there's nothing to be done.
  • 诸客皆述所好以进 - All the guests described their likes and presented them.
  • 吕悉见惯不鲜 - Lu found them all too familiar and uninteresting.
  • 一客忽附耳笑而云云 - One guest suddenly whispered something to Lu and laughed.
  • 吕鼓掌曰：“可以作一日消遣矣。” - Lu clapped his hands and said, 'This can be a way to pass the day.'
  • 即致书各大官，便降西园，一赏海棠之盛 - He immediately wrote letters to all the officials, inviting them to descend to the Western Garden to enjoy the beauty of the crabapple flowers.
  • 当道诸公，非出其门下，即是吕门客 - The officials in power were either his disciples or his guests.
  • 故莫不承召而至 - Therefore, none failed to come when summoned.
  • 吕固屋宇宏深，迓客入三进，诸从者皆不得入 - Lu's house was vast and deep, and he welcomed the guests into the third courtyard, while the attendants were not allowed to enter.
  • 惟主宾数人，东西升降而已 - Only the host and a few guests moved back and forth.
  • 至园中，但见海棠十树，红如车盖 - When they reached the garden, they saw ten crabapple trees, red as chariot canopies.
  • 树下群女百辈，皆短衣团绣，执刀雁行立 - Under the trees, a hundred women stood in formation, dressed in short clothes with embroidered patterns, holding knives in a line like geese.
  • 客曰：“何用女戎？” - The guest asked, 'Why use female warriors?'
  • 吕曰：“非此不可以为欢” - Lu replied, 'Without them, there would be no enjoyment.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 羊脂玉笛 - A jade flute made of white jade, symbolizing high quality and elegance.
  • 席间 - During a banquet or social gathering.
  • 春愁 - Spring sorrow, a common theme in Chinese literature expressing melancholy during spring.
  • 当道诸公 - Officials in power, often referring to those who hold significant political influence.
  • 吕门客 - Guests or disciples of Lu, indicating a close relationship or network.
  • 车盖 - A chariot canopy, used here as a metaphor for the vibrant color of the crabapple flowers.
  • 女戎 - Female warriors, a reference to the practice of using women in military roles, which was less common in traditional Chinese society.

Proper Nouns

  • 吕 - Lu, the host of the event.
  • 羊脂玉笛 - A jade flute made of white jade.
  • 海棠 - Crabapple, a type of flower.
  • 当道诸公 - Officials in power.
  • 西园 - The Western Garden, the location of the event.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 登堂设皋比 - Formal setting for a gathering or ceremony; '登堂' means to ascend the hall, '皋比' is a type of tiger skin used as a seat or mat.
  • 呂踞坐 - Sit in a relaxed or informal manner, possibly indicating a lack of respect or formality.
  • 命免冠 - Formal command to remove one's headwear, often a sign of respect or submission; '命' means to order, '免冠' means to remove the hat.
  • 更巾帼衣 - Change into women's attire; '更' means to change, '巾帼' refers to women's headscarves or headdresses, '衣' means clothing.
  • 歌以侑觞 - Singing to accompany drinking; '歌' means to sing, '侑' means to accompany or enhance, '觞' refers to drinking vessels or the act of drinking.
  • 老夫亦聊借为娱耳 - Using the activity for casual entertainment; '老夫' is a modest term for an older man, '聊' means to casually or briefly, '借' means to borrow or use, '为' means for, '娱' means entertainment, '耳' softens the statement.
  • 幸勿罪 - Polite request not to be blamed or punished; '幸' means please, '勿' means do not, '罪' means blame or punish.
  • 大设醴酺 - Grand feast with sweet wine; '大设' means to set up grandly, '醴' refers to a type of sweet wine, '酺' means a feast.
  • 群切齿共谋吕氏 - Plotting against the Lu family; '群' means all, '切齿' means to gnash teeth (indicating anger or resentment), '共' means together, '谋' means to plot, '吕氏' refers to the family or followers of Lu.
  • 不移晷也 - A very short period of time, emphasizing the quickness of the plotting; '不移' means not moving, '晷' refers to the shadow of a sundial, symbolizing a short period of time.
  • 其事皆寝 - Abandoning or forgetting the matters, indicating a lack of follow-through or action; '其事' means those matters, '寝' means to abandon or put aside.
  • 已载厚币往 - Carrying rich gifts to the place, indicating a bribe or offering; '已' means already, '载' means to carry, '厚币' means rich gifts or heavy bribes, '往' means to go.
  • 候其出而赂之 - Waiting for them to leave and then bribing them, indicating a strategic move to avoid detection; '候' means to wait, '其出' means when they leave, '赂' means to bribe.
  • 及宴归，而金已在笥矣 - After the banquet, the gold was already in the box, indicating a successful bribe; '及' means when, '宴归' means after the banquet, '而' is a conjunction, '金' means gold or money, '已' means already, '笥' means a box or a chest.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 吕氏 - Refers to the family or followers of Lu, a historical figure or group.
  • 季文子 - Refers to Ji Wenzi, a historical figure known for his integrity and dignity, often cited in classical texts as an example of proper conduct.
  • 醴 - A type of sweet wine, often associated with feasts and celebrations in ancient China.
  • 巾帼 - Traditionally refers to women's headscarves or headdresses, but can also symbolize femininity or disguise.
  • 免冠 - Removing one's hat or cap, a gesture of respect or submission, often seen in formal settings.
  • 歌以侑觞 - Singing to accompany drinking, a common practice in ancient Chinese banquets and social gatherings.
  • 大设醴酺 - A grand feast with sweet wine, often held to celebrate important events or to entertain guests.
  • 群切齿共谋吕氏 - Plotting against the Lu family, indicating a sense of collective resentment or anger.
  • 不移晷也 - A very short period of time, emphasizing the quickness of the plotting.
  • 其事皆寝 - Abandoning or forgetting the matters, indicating a lack of follow-through or action.
  • 已载厚币往 - Carrying rich gifts to the place, indicating a bribe or offering.
  • 候其出而赂之 - Waiting for them to leave and then bribing them, indicating a strategic move to avoid detection.
  • 及宴归，而金已在笥矣 - After the banquet, the gold was already in the box, indicating a successful bribe.

Proper Nouns

  • 吕氏 - The family or followers of Lu.
  • 季文子 - Ji Wenzi, a historical figure known for his integrity and dignity.
  • 司马白公 - Si Ma Bai Gong, a person identified as the one who sang the soft, gentle tune.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 游瞰江山 - To roam and gaze at the rivers and mountains, meaning to travel and appreciate the natural scenery.
  • 置酒临江 - To place wine by the river, meaning to hold a banquet by the river.
  • 玩其迷漫炫烂之景 - To enjoy the bewilderingly splendid scene, meaning to admire the dazzling and intricate view.
  • 号为金雪 - To call it golden snow, a poetic name for the scene created by the gold foil.
  • 自辰及申 - From the hour of Chen to the hour of Shen, referring to the time period from morning to late afternoon.
  • 霏霏不止 - Continuing to fall gently, describing the ongoing scattering of gold foil.
  • 如吕氏之所为 - Like what Lord Lü did, referring to the actions of Lord Lü.
  • 岂吕氏之所能自为 - Is it something Lord Lü could do by himself? Suggesting that the action was beyond Lord Lü's personal capability.
  • 盖诚有大力者驱而为之 - It is indeed a powerful force that drives it, implying a supernatural or divine intervention.
  • 深明夫聚敛附益之为 - Deeply revealing the act of accumulating and increasing, criticizing the practice of hoarding wealth.
  • 作牛马于儿孙者 - Making one's descendants work like oxen and horses, criticizing those who burden their descendants with hard labor.
  • 徒为多事 - Just causing trouble, meaning that such actions are unnecessary and troublesome.
  • 吕氏之散金游戏 - Lord Lü's game of scattering gold, referring to Lord Lü's act of distributing gold.
  • 其智不在中人下 - His wisdom is not inferior to that of an average person, suggesting that Lord Lü is intelligent.
  • 说者多愚之 - Many commentators consider him foolish, indicating that some people think Lord Lü is foolish.
  • 孰愚孰智 - Who is foolish and who is wise, a rhetorical question asking who is more intelligent.
  • 必有能辨之者 - There must be someone who can distinguish this, suggesting that there are people who can make a clear judgment.
  • 励志好修 - Determined to cultivate virtue, describing a person who is committed to self-improvement.
  • 尔室不愧 - Your room is not ashamed, meaning that one's home is honorable and respectable.
  • 真君子儒也 - Truly a gentleman-scholar, praising a person who is both a scholar and a gentleman.
  • 刚正不阿 - Upright and unbending, describing a person who is honest and principled.
  • 妻子来任所 - His wife and children come to his post, referring to the arrival of his family to his place of work.
  • 公不许入城 - He does not allow them to enter the city, indicating that he does not permit his family to enter the city.
  • 赋诗以却之 - He writes a poem to refuse them, meaning that he writes a poem to politely decline his family's entry into the city.
  • 看罢家书意惘然 - After reading the family letters, feeling bewildered, describing a state of confusion after reading letters from home.
  • 纷纷相劝置庄田 - They urge him to buy land and property, indicating that his family encourages him to invest in land.
  • 狼山不卷千年画 - Wolf Mountain does not roll up its thousand-year-old painting, a metaphor for the enduring beauty of the landscape.
  • 鸡水新栽五亩莲 - Chicken Water newly planted with five acres of lotus, describing a new addition to the landscape.
  • 击鼓登堂真说法 - Striking the drum and ascending the hall to truly speak the law, a metaphor for giving a lecture or teaching.
  • 燃灯独坐类参禅 - Lighting a lamp and sitting alone, like meditating, describing a solitary and contemplative state.
  • 囊空犹是当年我 - My purse is still empty, just like it was in the past, indicating that he remains poor.
  • 未许妻儿索俸钱 - I do not allow my wife and children to ask for salary money, meaning that he does not provide financial support to his family.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 吕氏 - Refers to Lord Lü, a historical figure known for his extravagant and sometimes controversial actions.
  • 国朝邵嗣尧 - Refers to the official title and era of Sin Shih-yao, a historical figure known for his integrity and dedication to public service.
  • 山西人 - Indicates that Sin Shih-yao is from Shanxi province.
  • 庚戌进士 - Refers to the year he passed the imperial examination, a significant achievement in Chinese history.

Proper Nouns

  • 吕氏 - Lord Lü, a historical figure known for his extravagant and sometimes controversial actions.
  • 国朝邵嗣尧 - Sin Shih-yao, a historical figure known for his integrity and dedication to public service.
  • 山西人 - Shanxi province, the region from which Sin Shih-yao originates.
  • 庚戌进士 - The year Sin Shih-yao passed the imperial examination, a significant achievement in Chinese history.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 妻子閱詩 - Literally 'wife and children read poetry,' but likely metaphorical, meaning the wife (and possibly the family) engages in literary or scholarly activities.
  • 仍歸故里 - 'Still return to the old hometown,' indicating a return to one’s origins.
  • 擢御史 - A standard bureaucratic promotion term.
  • 崇祀 - To be honored with a shrine or memorial.
  • 合卺之夕 - Classical term referring to the wedding night.
  • 余视之皆客寄也 - I see them as guests staying temporarily. This metaphor implies a sense of detachment or observation from the author.
  • 顺治辛卯举于乡，癸巳进士 - Standard terms for the imperial examination system.
  • 檄署 - A formal order to take a position.
  • 甫入署 - Indicates immediate action upon arriving.
  • 斋夫 - May be a term for attendants or assistants in the school.
  • 丰范 - Refers to appearance and bearing.
  • 道故 - To speak of old times.
  • 瘐死 - Dying in prison due to harsh conditions.
  • 恤 - To show compassion or pity.
  • 置田宅 - To provide land and housing.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 邵士梅 - The name of the person being profiled, likely a scholar-official of the Qing dynasty.
  • 字峄晖 - His courtesy name, 'Yihui,' used in classical Chinese biographies.
  • 济宁 - A city in Shandong Province, indicating his birthplace.
  • 顺治辛卯 - The year 1651 (the 12th year of the Shunzhi Emperor’s reign).
  • 癸巳 - The year 1653 (the 14th year of the Shunzhi Emperor’s reign).
  • 登州 - A prefecture in Shandong, where he was appointed as a professor.

  • 北直名宦祠 - A shrine in the Northern Circuit (Beizhi) dedicated to distinguished officials. '北直' refers to the northern region under the jurisdiction of the imperial court.
  • 高东海 - A person mentioned as having died in prison, likely a former acquaintance of Shao.
  • 倾囊代赎 - To empty one’s purse to redeem, referring to paying a debt or ransom.
  • 私一娼 - To have a relationship with a courtesan, indicating an extramarital affair.
  • 坐隐盗 - To be implicated in a hidden theft, suggesting that the courtesan was accused of a crime.
  • 恤其子 - Show compassion to his son, indicating the moral obligation of the scholar-official to help the family of a deceased acquaintance.

Proper Nouns

  • 邵士梅 - The main subject, a Qing dynasty scholar-official.

  • 顺治 - The reign title of the Shunzhi Emperor of the Qing dynasty.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 后邵妻病笃 - 后邵的妻子病重，表示她即将去世。
  • 又将别矣 - 表示他们将再次分别，暗示后邵的妻子即将离世。
  • 死当生馆陶董家 - 预言后邵的妻子死后会转世到馆陶的董家。
  • 所居滨河河曲第三家 - 描述房子的位置，位于河湾处的第三户人家。
  • 君异时官罢后，萧寺繙经，尚当重结丝罗也 - 预言后邵将来会在寺庙中读经，并与那位女子再次相遇，象征着“重结丝罗”，即重新结缘。
  • 已而迁吴江知县，谢病归 - 表示后邵后来升任吴江县令，因病辞官回家。
  • 家居无聊 - 表示后邵在家感到无聊。
  • 同年某为馆陶令 - 表示后邵的同年（同科进士）担任馆陶的县令。
  • 垂髫女约十五六 - 表示一个大约十五六岁的少女。
  • 对邵若有低徊之意 - 表示那个女孩似乎对后邵有留恋之意。
  • 果董姓 - 确认那个女孩确实是董家的人。

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 萧寺 - 指佛教寺庙，常用于古典文学中表示修行或静修之地。
  • 繙经 - 指读经，表示一种宗教或学术活动。
  • 丝罗 - 比喻浪漫或婚姻关系，常用于古典文学中表示深厚的情感联系。
  • 同年 - 指同科进士，表示学者官员之间的专业和社会联系。
  • 馆陶 - 地名，可能指古代中国的某个地区或城镇。

Proper Nouns

  • 后邵 - 主角的名字。
  • 馆陶 - 地名。
  • 宝相寺 - 寺庙的名字。
  • 董 - 预言中提到的家族姓氏。

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 不字人 - Literally 'not to be married,' but in this context, it means the woman has not yet married.
  • 觌面时 - Means 'when they first met' or 'upon meeting.'
  • 一见如故 - A common idiom meaning 'to feel as if they had known each other for a long time.'
  • 忘年交 - Refers to a friendship between people of different ages.
  • 岳氏未育 - Means 'the wife (岳氏) had not given birth.'
  • 固盟而逝 - Means 'to swear an oath and then pass away.'
  • 一再至三 - Means 'from one to two, and then to three,' referring to the rarity of such a situation.
  • 从古罕有 - Means 'rare from ancient times.'
  • 无疾终 - Means 'to die without illness.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 董姓 - Refers to the surname 'Dong,' indicating the family background of the woman.
  • 济宁邵进士 - Refers to a man named Shao from Jining who passed the imperial examination (进士).
  • 襄阳城王氏门前有两柳树者 - Refers to a specific location in Xiangyang (Xiangyang City) where there are two willow trees in front of a Wang family's gate.
  • 月老红丝 - Refers to the mythological figure Yue Lao, who ties the red threads that bind couples together in marriage.

Proper Nouns

  • 董姓 - The surname 'Dong.'
  • 济宁邵进士 - A man named Shao from Jining who passed the imperial examination.
  • 襄阳城王氏门前有两柳树者 - A specific location in Xiangyang with two willow trees in front of a Wang family's gate.
  • 月老 - The mythological figure Yue Lao, who ties the red threads that bind couples together in marriage.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 及笄 - Refers to a girl reaching the age of marriageability, typically around 15 years old.
  • 求婚者日盈于门 - Literally means 'suitors fill the door every day,' indicating a high volume of marriage proposals.
  • 严拒之 - Means 'refused sternly,' suggesting a firm rejection.
  • 随父母游岘山归 - Means 'returning with parents from a trip to Xian Mountain.'
  • 伫立，攀条泫然 - Describes the young man standing still, touching the branches of the willow trees, and shedding tears, indicating a deep emotional state.
  • 啖以果馅 - Means 'fed with fruit and pastry,' showing hospitality.
  • 卿怎不似馆陶重会时乎 - A rhetorical question asking, 'Why don't you look like the time we met again in Guantao?' This suggests a past encounter that left a strong impression.
  • 不料郎君已再世矣 - Means 'I didn't expect you to have passed away,' expressing surprise and sadness.
  • 相与痛哭 - Means 'they both wept bitterly,' indicating a shared emotional moment.
  • 由是公子日夜号泣 - Means 'since then, the young man wept day and night,' showing his intense grief.
  • 思念王氏 - Means 'thinking of the Wang family,' indicating his longing for the girl.
  • 父母以王氏长七岁，不愿婚 - Means 'the parents, considering the Wang girl was seven years older, did not wish to marry them,' showing a concern about the age difference.
  • 父母不得已从焉 - Means 'the parents had no choice but to agree,' indicating their eventual concession.
  • 十五而娶，女已年二十有二 - Means 'he married at fifteen, while the girl was already twenty-two,' highlighting the significant age difference.

  • 窦娥儿惹下飞霜禾尽打 - Refers to the story of Dou E, a character from Yuan dynasty literature, who was wrongfully executed and caused a snowstorm as a result of her injustice. This alludes to the theme of wrongful suffering and divine retribution.
  • 新息侯薏苡明珠真乱假 - Refers to the story of Xinxihou, who was falsely accused of bringing back pearls made of rice grains (薏苡) from a military campaign. This alludes to the theme of false accusations and political intrigue.
  • 台空铜雀犹留瓦 - Refers to the ruins of the Copper Bird Terrace, a symbol of the decline of the Han dynasty. '犹留瓦' suggests that even in ruins, remnants of the past remain.
  • 汉淮阴求一饭，甘心胯下 - Refers to the story of Han Xin, a general who endured humiliation by allowing a man to kick him in the crotch in exchange for food. This alludes to the theme of enduring hardship for future success.
  • 陶彭泽五斗腰叉 - Refers to Tao Yuanming, a famous poet who resigned from office because he could not tolerate being paid with only five dou of rice. '五斗腰叉' symbolizes his disdain for low pay and his preference for a simple life.
  • 壶觞市不赊，吃不起蹠鸡臛鸭 - '壶觞' (wine and cups) and '蹠鸡臛鸭' (a dish made from chicken and duck) are used to describe the speaker's inability to afford even basic luxuries.
  • 春衣典没些，穿不上绣裙罗袜 - '春衣典没些' (spring clothes pawned away) and '穿不上绣裙罗袜' (unable to wear embroidered skirts and silk socks) describe the speaker's poverty and lack of clothing.
  • 一任他江山锦片前程大，争似俺风雪单寒处士家 - '一任他江山锦片前程大' (let others enjoy their grand prospects) and '争似俺风雪单寒处士家' (how much better is our humble life in the cold) contrast the speaker's modest lifestyle with the grandeur of others.
  • 何须论金谷繁华，玉堂声价 - '金谷繁华' (the opulence of Jin Valley) and '玉堂声价' (the prestige of the jade hall) refer to the luxurious and prestigious lives of the elite, which the speaker finds unnecessary.

  • 把闷弓儿且按下，莫管他风月在谁家 - '闷弓儿' (a metaphor for a troubled heart) and '风月在谁家' (who owns the moon and flowers) suggest letting go of personal worries and not caring about others' affairs.
  • 且放开笑和尚的布袋，丢了那莽八戒的钉钯 - '笑和尚的布袋' (the laughing monk's bag) and '莽八戒的钉钯' (the clumsy Bajie's pickaxe) are metaphors for letting go of burdens and responsibilities.
  • 不平事莫问咱，一谜价妆聋做哑 - '不平事莫问咱' (don't ask about unfair things) and '一谜价妆聋做哑' (pretend to be deaf and mute) suggest ignoring injustice and staying silent.
  • 写一部天花，学一尊菩萨 - '写一部天花' (write a book of heavenly flowers) and '学一尊菩萨' (learn from a Buddha) suggest engaging in spiritual or literary pursuits.
  • 但愿他没一个冤家，好人满天下 - '没一个冤家' (no enemies) and '好人满天下' (good people everywhere) express a wish for a peaceful and virtuous world.
  • 只就我吊古扳今，斩鬼封神平妖怪，都是逢人劝化 - '吊古扳今' (reflecting on the past and present) and '斩鬼封神平妖怪' (slaying demons, sealing gods, and pacifying monsters) suggest engaging in heroic or spiritual deeds, all while trying to guide others.

  • 盖东西两粤扶舆之脉，蕴结而成 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'the convergence of the mountain ranges of the eastern and western Guangdong regions, formed through the accumulation of energy or qi'; '扶舆' refers to the movement or flow of qi (vital energy).
  • 欧阳文忠亦以“精石”目之也 - Ouyang Wenzhong also called it 'jing shi' (precious stone); reference to Ouyang Xiu, a famous Song dynasty scholar and official.

  • 值中丞李文介开采旧坑 - 'When Governor Li Wenjie was excavating old quarries'; '中丞' is a title for a provincial official; '开采旧坑' refers to the excavation of old stone pits.
  • 见其董率工事，因得其概 - 'I saw him overseeing the work, and thus gained a general understanding'; '董率' means to supervise or direct.
  • 南屏沈子贻砚，所为旧蕉白 - 'The inkstone from Nanping, given by Shen Ziye, was made from old jiaobai stone'; '旧蕉白' is a specific type of stone used for inkstones.
  • 信是“康子洞”前之产，非时代物 - 'It is indeed from the area before Kangzi Cave, not a product of later times'; statement of provenance.
  • 欣喜过望，遂忆往事，爰笔为记 - 'I was overjoyed, and thus recalled past events, and wrote this down'; '爰笔为记' is a classical expression meaning 'thus I wrote it down'.
  • 时在嘉庆二年春，客汶上馆中 - 'At that time, it was spring of the second year of Jiaqing, while I was a guest in the residence of Wen Shang'; '嘉庆二年' corresponds to 1801 in the Gregorian calendar.
  • 段子崄 - A place name, likely referring to a village or area named Duanzixian.

  • 遇大雪飘飘如掌，峰峦玉琢，野甸银铺 - Poetic description of a heavy snowfall: 'When a heavy snow fell like a palm, the peaks were carved from jade, and the fields were covered in silver'.

  • 曷归，将谋晚炊 - 'Why not return? We must plan for dinner'; '曷' is an archaic particle meaning 'why not'.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 工向暖，以两手虚探其上 - Literal description of the worker warming himself and tentatively reaching out, possibly with a sense of caution or uncertainty.
  • 热秫浆斟工曰：“饮一杯荡风雪。” - 热秫浆 refers to warm millet porridge or gruel, a traditional warming drink. 荡风雪 is a metaphorical expression meaning to dispel the cold or chill, suggesting comfort or relief.
  • 工接杯自酌 - 自酌 means to drink alone, implying a moment of solitude or self-reflection.
  • 目曼视工 - 目曼视 describes a gaze that is both curious and perhaps flirtatious, indicating a close or intimate look.
  • 我某姓老孀也，止一女，未有婿家 - 老孀 refers to an elderly widow, and 止一女 means she has only one daughter. This introduces a sense of loneliness and the desire for a family.
  • 尽半子职 - 尽半子职 means to fulfill the duties of a son, implying a deep commitment and care.
  • 你终身薄粥，可不劳咄嗟也 - 终身薄粥 suggests a life of modest means, and 可不劳咄嗟也 means you won't have to worry about daily sustenance, indicating a promise of support.
  • 蒙姥姥不嫌，实所厚感 - 蒙姥姥不嫌 means to be accepted by the grandmother-in-law without prejudice, and 实所厚感 expresses deep gratitude.
  • 但我无一钱，归告兄嫂，为我一番打算 - 无一钱 means having no money, and 为我一番打算 means to make plans for me, indicating a request for assistance.
  • 吾为尔室家，以攸宁尔，岂于尔顶上加愁帽耶 - 吾为尔室家 means I will provide you with a home, and 岂于尔顶上加愁帽耶 is a rhetorical question meaning how could I add more worry to your life? This phrase emphasizes the grandmother-in-law's benevolence and concern for the worker's well-being.

  • 尽半子职 - Fulfilling the duties of a son, which in traditional Chinese culture often involved taking care of one's parents and maintaining the family lineage.
  • 终身薄粥 - A life of modest means, reflecting the economic conditions and social expectations of the time.
